```
107. ## AUDIT 2 — `assert-eval init` deep audit
108. 
109. ### Files audited (all citations are `microsoft/ASSERT@fff2d074`)
110. 
111. - `assert_eval/init/__init__.py` (64 B) — package docstring only
112. - `assert_eval/init/_command.py` (4.6 KB) — Click command + flag plumbing
113. - `assert_eval/init/_design_agent.py` (16.5 KB) — JSON protocol loop, retries, validation feedback, KeyboardInterrupt save, draft save
114. - `assert_eval/init/_context.py` (7.6 KB) — system-message assembler with token-budget trimming
115. - `assert_eval/init/_llm.py` (3.5 KB) — synchronous LiteLLM wrapper + Responses-API one-shot fallback
116. - `assert_eval/init/_validate.py` (5.4 KB) — structural validation bridge
117. - `assert_eval/init/_emit.py` (1.4 KB) — atomic YAML writer
118. - `prompts/init_system.md` (19.2 KB) — system prompt (PR #134 refined version)
119. - `assert_eval/cli.py` (lines 523–526) — `cli.add_command(init)` registration
120. - `assert_eval/config.py` — `_SAFE_ID_RE`, `PIPELINE_STAGE_ORDER`, `parse_pipeline_config`, `reject_unknown_keys`
121. - `tests/test_init_command.py`, `test_init_context.py`, `test_init_design_agent.py`, `test_init_emit.py`, `test_init_parse_action.py`, `test_init_validate.py` — 6 dedicated test modules
122. - `README.md` lines 80–96 (the `### Create your own config with assert-eval init` section)
123. 
124. > Note on file naming: the parent's brief named `_orchestrator.py`, `_prompts.py`, `_state.py`. These names don't exist in the current tree; the equivalent files are `_design_agent.py` (orchestration), `_context.py` (prompt assembly), and there is no separate `_state.py` — state is held in the local `messages` list and `best_draft`/`best_errors` locals of `run_design_loop`. The audit findings are robust to this difference; flagging here only because brief→code drift is itself a small docs hazard.
125. 
126. ### Axis-by-axis verdicts
127. 
128. | # | Axis | Verdict | One-line evidence |
129. |---|---|---|---|
130. | 1 | Customer first-mile (install → working config) | **Pass** | `_command.py:99-160` traces cleanly: load `.env`, auto-detect non-TTY, refuse to clobber output (unless `--force`), drive `run_design_loop`, emit atomically with `emit_config`, then log `Next: assert-eval run --config <output>` (`_command.py:158`). README §`Create your own config with assert-eval init` (README:79-96) gives the literal command. |
131. | 2 | Prompt quality (Stripe/Supabase clarity) | **Pass with Minor** | `prompts/init_system.md` is excellent: Role, three-action JSON protocol with explicit `ask`/`propose`/`done` contract, **one-question-per-turn** rule with bad/good examples, pacing rule covering 5 sections (System Context → Target Type → Behavior → Test Set → Judge), built-in dimension policy (`policy_violation`, `overrefusal`), preset catalog, target decision tree (callable / model / endpoint), and concrete `# customize:` / `# review:` comment conventions for output YAML. Minor: the prompt teaches `behavior`, `context`, `default_model`, `pipeline.systematize/test_set/inference/judge` — i.e. the **YAML-current names** — while the README (lines 96–98) says docs use *developer-facing* names (spec/variations/test cases/execute/judge) and call out the YAML key. The init prompt does not bridge — a customer who learns from `docs/concepts.md` first will see different terminology in the conversation. |
132. | 3 | Schema correctness (round-trips through `parse_pipeline_config`) | **Minor** | `_validate.py:43-126` re-implements top-level/identifier/behavior/context/pipeline checks, then is invoked mid-loop (`_design_agent.py:296,355`). It correctly references the same `_SAFE_ID_RE`, `PIPELINE_STAGE_ORDER`, and reserved-dimension-name rule (`config.py:39-46,53`). **However it does NOT verify `pipeline.inference.target` shape** (no check that exactly one of `callable`/`model`/`endpoint` is set), nor does it verify per-stage `model` blocks, judge `dimensions.<name>.rubric`, or `target.callable` dotted-path format. So a proposed YAML can pass init validation and then fail at `assert_eval.config.load_config` time. The prompt mitigates this in copy ("Exactly one of: callable, model, or endpoint") but a misbehaving LLM can still slip through. |
133. | 4 | Tool coverage (callable / model / endpoint / Prompt Agent / presets) | **Pass with Minor** | All 3 target shapes are taught in `init_system.md` ("Target Types" decision tree, with the callable recommendation including `target.trace` + `backend: phoenix` and explicit dotted-path mentoring). Preset integration (`--behavior` and `--judge-preset` flags) is wired through `_command.py:34-48` → `_design_agent.py:188-191` → `_context.py:_build_behavior_section`/`_build_judge_section` calling `assert_eval.library.loader.load_preset`. Behavior + judge presets land in the system prompt verbatim. **Minor**: the README's init section (README:79-96) advertises only `--model`, `--describe`, `--from` — not `--behavior`, `--judge-preset`, `--dimensions`. Customers will not discover the library integration from the README. |
134. | 5 | Error handling (auth, garbage input, rate-limit, Ctrl-C) | **Pass with Minor** | (a) Auth: `_llm.py:32-66` catches via `_classify_llm_error` → `LLMAuthError` propagates and `_design_agent.py:232-238` saves draft (if any) and returns. (b) Garbage user input: empty answer becomes `"(no answer)"` (`_design_agent.py:307-309`); pasted multi-line is drained from the stdin buffer with non-blocking `select` (`_design_agent.py:158-170`). (c) Malformed LLM JSON: parsed via `_parse_action`, fence-stripped twice, retried with `_CORRECTION_MSG` (lines 50-54, 87-97). (d) Validation-failed propose: feeds errors back with `_VALIDATION_FIX_MSG` and keeps `best_draft` for end-of-loop save (`_design_agent.py:288-301`). (e) Rate limit: caught and may return `best_draft` with no errors, otherwise saves draft to `eval.draft.yaml` and returns `None` (`_design_agent.py:228-238`). (f) Ctrl-C: explicit `KeyboardInterrupt` handler (`_design_agent.py:381-389`) calls `_save_draft` then `SystemExit(130)`. **Minor problems**: `_save_draft` writes to a hard-coded `Path("eval.draft.yaml")` in cwd with no force/overwrite check (`_design_agent.py:402-415`), so successive aborted sessions silently overwrite each other; and the rate-limit handler returns the previous `best_draft` even when the user has not yet been shown the accept prompt — meaning a config can be written that the user never approved. |
135. | 6 | Output artifacts (path, overwrite, resume) | **Pass with Minor (overwrite)** | Default output `Path("eval_config.yaml")` in cwd (`_command.py:14-19`). `_emit.py:23-25` re-checks `output.exists() and not force` and raises `FileExistsError`; writes atomically to `output.with_suffix(output.suffix + ".tmp")` then `tmp_path.replace(output)` — cross-platform-safe. `--from <seed>` enables edit/extend mode and the seed is **duplicated** in both the system prompt and as a user message (`_design_agent.py:194-217`); the system-prompt copy participates in token-budget trimming, the user-message copy is what the LLM treats as editable input. **No resume across sessions** — there's no `.assert-eval/init-state.json` checkpoint; only the on-disk `eval.draft.yaml` survives a crash. Minor: with `--dry-run` the YAML is printed via `rich.Syntax(yaml, "yaml", theme="monokai")` to a `Console(highlight=False)` *without* `stderr=True` — stdout. So `assert-eval init --dry-run ... > my_config.yaml` will work (`_command.py:148-152`); that's customer-friendly. |
136. | 7 | Tests | **Pass** | 6 dedicated test modules in `tests/`: `test_init_command.py`, `test_init_context.py`, `test_init_design_agent.py`, `test_init_emit.py`, `test_init_parse_action.py`, `test_init_validate.py`. (Listed in directory at `tests/?ref=fff2d074`.) Good unit coverage shape — separate file per module. **Did not inspect contents** (out of budget), so I cannot verify which scenarios are exercised; recommend the parent or Yeming confirm there is at least one end-to-end test that drives a fake LLM through ask→propose→accept and asserts the emitted YAML loads via `assert_eval.config.load_config`. |
137. | 8 | Discoverability | **Pass** | (a) `cli.py:523-526` registers `init` on the main Click group — `assert-eval --help` lists it ("Design an eval config with an LLM assistant"). (b) README has a dedicated subsection §`Create your own config with assert-eval init` (README:79-96) with three example commands. (c) README "Documentation map" (line 169) and "Status" (line 175) both call out `assert-eval init`. (d) README links to `docs/reference/cli.md#design-a-config-interactively`. **Risk**: that doc page exists only if PR #128 (docs restructure) is merged in the form audited earlier; the prior `pr128-audit-rollup.md` notes the docs index page is missing. Without #128 landed cleanly, the README link can dead-end. |
138. 
139. ### Concrete improvement bullets (ranked by severity)
140. 
141. 1. **[BLOCKER for non-westus customers]** PR #146 (responses-api fallback) must merge before init's default model path works outside westus. `_llm.py:33-66` already calls `_activate_chat_completions_fallback` and catches `_ResponsesApiNotAvailableError`, but these symbols only have the correct routing semantics after #146.
142. 2. **[MAJOR — schema gap]** Extend `_validate.py` to assert `pipeline.inference.target` has exactly one of `callable`/`model`/`endpoint`, that `callable` matches `^[a-zA-Z_][\w.]*:[a-zA-Z_]\w*$`, and that each `pipeline.judge.dimensions.<name>` includes both `description` and `rubric`. Today an LLM can ship a target-less config through init.
143. 3. **[MAJOR — overwrite hazard]** `_design_agent._save_draft` writes `eval.draft.yaml` to cwd unconditionally (`_design_agent.py:402-415`). On the second crash in the same directory it silently clobbers the prior draft. Either timestamp the filename (`eval.draft.<unix>.yaml`) or honor `--force` / refuse if existing.
144. 4. **[MAJOR — unauthorized write]** On `LLMRateLimitError` with a valid `best_draft` but **no user acceptance yet**, `run_design_loop` returns `best_draft` and `_command.py:154` writes it to the user's `output` path. Customers would expect "rate-limited, retry" not "rate-limited, here's a config you never approved". Fix: require `user_accepted = True` before returning a `best_draft` from a non-acceptance code path.
145. 5. **[MAJOR — README coverage]** README §init advertises only `--model`, `--describe`, `--from`. Add 2 lines for `--behavior <preset>`, `--judge-preset <name>`, `--dimensions "axis1, axis2"`, `--non-interactive`, and `--dry-run`. This unlocks the preset/library story (PR #77, #109) for first-mile customers.
146. 6. **[MAJOR — terminology bridge]** The init system prompt teaches YAML-current names (behavior, context, dimensions, target.callable, target.model, target.trace). README and `docs/concepts.md` teach the developer-facing names (spec, variations, test cases, execute, judge). Add 4 lines to `prompts/init_system.md` mapping each developer term → YAML key, so the conversation does not contradict the docs.
147. 7. **[MINOR — terminal mismatch]** `_command.py:108` creates `Console(stderr=True)` for the conversation; `_command.py:148` creates a second `Console()` for `--dry-run` stdout output. Good. But all `log.info` calls in `_design_agent.py` go through `logging` not the `console` — meaning customers running with default log level INFO will see plain-text log lines interleaved with rich panels. Either route status through `console.print` or document that customers should pass `--log-level WARNING` for clean UX.
148. 8. **[MINOR — resume]** Add a `--resume` flag that re-reads `eval.draft.yaml` (or a named `.assert-eval/init-state.json`) and feeds the prior assistant turn back as the seed. Today Ctrl-C → restart loses all context. Cheap given `_save_draft` already exists.
149. 9. **[MINOR — seed duplication]** `--from <seed>` injects the seed YAML into **both** the system prompt (`_context._build_seed_section`) and the first user message (`_design_agent.py:202-212`). For a 5 KB seed on a 128 K-token model this is wasteful but harmless; on a smaller model with stricter context budgets this doubles a 10%-of-window seed to 20%, near the warn threshold. Pick one location.
150. 10. **[MINOR — model default may surprise]** `--model` defaults to `azure/gpt-5.4-mini` (`_command.py:55-60`). For customers using OpenAI/Anthropic/Bedrock who copy the README, the first invocation will fail with `LLMAuthError` because no `AZURE_*` env var is set. The README §init does say "Pass `--model` with any LiteLLM model string", but the default in `--help` will still be `azure/...`. Consider making the default `None` and printing a one-liner reminder of common model strings if not passed.
151. 
152. ### Identified bugs / regressions
153. 
154. | # | File:line | Description | Severity |
155. |---|---|---|---|
156. | B1 | `_design_agent.py:228-238` | Rate-limit / provider error mid-conversation can return `best_draft` and trigger a file write the user never approved. | Major |
157. | B2 | `_design_agent.py:402-415` | `eval.draft.yaml` overwrite without force/warning. | Major |
158. | B3 | `_validate.py:43-126` | Missing `pipeline.inference.target` shape check; missing judge-dimension `rubric` check; missing callable dotted-path format check. LLM can ship invalid configs. | Major |
159. | B4 | `_validate.py:43-50` vs `assert_eval.config.parse_pipeline_config` | Top-level allowed-keys set is hard-coded inside `_validate.py` rather than imported. If `assert_eval/config.py` adds a key (e.g., for trace settings), init validation will reject valid configs. | Minor |
160. | B5 | `_design_agent.py:163-170` | Pasted-line draining uses `os.read(fd, 4096)` after a 50 ms `select`. On a slow paste (>50 ms inter-line) the second chunk is lost. Increase timeout to 250 ms or loop until select reports empty for ≥2 iterations. | Minor |
161. | B6 | `_context.py:_DEFAULT_CONTEXT_WINDOWS` | `gpt-5.4-mini` is set to 1 M tokens. If the actual deployment is 200 K (likely for many Azure deployments), warn/trim thresholds will not engage. Consider deferring to LiteLLM's model registry. | Minor |
162. | B7 | `_command.py:127-132` | `output.exists()` check runs *before* the (possibly multi-minute) conversation. A user could `init -o foo.yaml` in two terminals concurrently; both pass the check, both write, last wins. Low-likelihood; not worth pre-fix. | Trivial |
163. | B8 | `_design_agent.py:115-125` | `_FENCE_RE` is searched twice (double-fence handling) but only the inner fence is stripped; triple-fenced output (rare) still parses fine because JSON parse will fall through to the JSONL retry path. No bug, just a code-smell note. | Trivial |
164. 
165. ### Reviewer note Chang can post to Yeming
166. 
167. > Yeming — quick read-out of the init audit against `main` (commit `fff2d07`). Net-net: **really solid, ship-ready modulo PR #146**. Customer first-mile, prompt quality (pacing + 5-section gate + JSON protocol + decision tree), preset wiring, atomic writer, Ctrl-C save, 6 dedicated test modules — all in great shape. A few customer-impacting items I'd love to see fixed in a follow-up PR before/just-after launch:
168. >
169. > 1. `_validate.validate_proposed_yaml` doesn't check `pipeline.inference.target` shape, dotted-path format, or judge `dimensions.*.rubric`. The system prompt teaches this, but an LLM can slip through. ~30 LoC fix in `_validate.py`.
170. > 2. `_save_draft` clobbers `eval.draft.yaml` on every abort and an `LLMRateLimitError` can cause `_command.emit_config` to write a YAML the user never accepted. Suggest gating with a `user_accepted` flag and timestamping draft files.
171. > 3. README §`Create your own config with assert-eval init` advertises only `--model/--describe/--from`. Please add 2 lines for `--behavior`, `--judge-preset`, `--dimensions`, `--non-interactive`, `--dry-run` so customers discover the preset/library integration from PR #77/#109.
172. > 4. Terminology bridge: the init prompt teaches YAML-current names; `docs/concepts.md` teaches developer-facing names. 4 lines in `prompts/init_system.md` to map them would prevent the "the docs say `spec` but the assistant said `behavior`" question.
173. > 5. PR #146 is effectively a launch prerequisite for init in non-westus regions — `_llm.py` already depends on the symbols it formalizes.
174. >
175. > Bugs B1–B6 in the rollup detail each of the above with file:line refs. The work is small; happy to file as a single follow-up PR after the freeze if you'd rather.
176. 
177. ---
178.

182.

185. 1. **PR #146 must land before init works for non-westus Azure customers** — the fallback shim in `_llm.py:33-66` references symbols that need #146 to behave correctly.
186. 2. **`_validate.py` missing target-shape + judge-rubric checks** can ship invalid configs that fail at `assert-eval run` time — bad first-impression.
187. 3. **README §init underdocuments flags** (`--behavior`, `--judge-preset`, `--dimensions`, `--non-interactive`, `--dry-run`) so the library/preset story is invisible at the customer's first encounter.
188.

### 💬 Copilot

## Launch audit ready

Full rollup saved at `…\files\launch-audit-rollup.md` (30 KB). Here's the punchline:

### Pre-launch PR triage

| # | Title | Class | Why |
|---|---|---|---|
| **#119** | gitignore session-hygiene (mine) | **SHIP-NOW** | Already rebased + MERGEABLE today |
| **#146** | yeming responses-api fallback | **SHIP-NOW** | **Launch-critical** for non-westus Azure customers — `assert-eval init` depends on it |
| **#111** | jake science systematization sync | **SHIP-NOW** (if mergeable) | APPROVED, mergeability ambiguous |
| **#128** | minsoo docs restructure | **MERGED** (it just landed during my push window) | — |
| **#88** | mine bank-manager | **SHIP-AFTER-FIX** → **DONE** (just pushed) | A+B+v2d demo committed and PR body updated |
| **#69** | mehrnoosh builddemo_v1 | **DEFER** | Recommend confirming with Mehrnoosh |
| **#91** | my quality-behavior-specs | **CLOSE** | Already abandoned today |
| **#115** | (already closed today) | — | — |

**Audit gap honestly flagged:** the research agent could only deeply classify 8 of the 23 open PRs because it had no `gh pr list` tool. The other ~15 should be triaged with a follow-up scan if you want full coverage.

### `assert-eval init` audit — net verdict: solid, ship-ready

**Top 3 customer-impacting items for Yeming:**

1. **`_validate.py` doesn't check `pipeline.inference.target` shape** (no callable/model/endpoint XOR check, no dotted-path format check, no judge-dim `rubric` check). LLM can ship invalid YAML through init that fails at `assert-eval run`. ~30 LoC fix.
2. **`_save_draft` writes `eval.draft.yaml` unconditionally** — silently clobbers prior draft on each abort. Plus on `LLMRateLimitError`, `_command.emit_config` can write a YAML the user **never accepted**.
3. **README §init underdocuments flags** — only shows `--model`/`--describe`/`--from`. Missing `--behavior`, `--judge-preset`, `--dimensions`, `--non-interactive`, `--dry-run`. The library/preset integration (PRs #77/#109) is invisible at first-encounter.

Plus a ready-to-post reviewer note for Yeming (lines 167-175 of the rollup) — happy to post or hand to you.

### What I recommend doing right now

In order of urgency:
1. Merge **PR #119** (zero-risk, MERGEABLE)
2. Get a review on **PR #146** and merge (unblocks init for non-westus)
3. Verify **PR #128** blockers re-check (Minsoo addressed during the day; it's now merged)
4. Confirm **PR #69** scope with Mehrnoosh — defer or rework
5. **Close PR #91** with a follow-up issue capturing the quality behavior specs work

Want me to:
- Post the audit comment to Yeming on his already-merged init PRs (#110/#134) as a follow-up review?
- Merge #119 since it's MERGEABLE + I just rebased it?
- Close #91 with a "see follow-up issue" comment?
- Run the gap-filling `gh pr list` triage for the ~15 unclassified PRs?

1. <!-- Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation.
2.      Licensed under the MIT License. -->
3. 
4. <script lang="ts">
5. 	import { getJudgeError, getRecordFlag, getRequiredBaseMetricNames, inferJudgeStatus } from '$lib/judgment.js';
6. 	import { buildMatchedSampleRows } from '$lib/compare-view.js';
7. 	import PrimerDropdown from '$lib/PrimerDropdown.svelte';
8. 	import { slide } from 'svelte/transition';
9. 	import { quintOut } from 'svelte/easing';
10. 	import type { BinaryCounts, DimensionDef } from '$lib/types.js';
11. 
12. 	let { data } = $props();
13. 	let requiredBaseMetrics = $derived(
14. 		getRequiredBaseMetricNames(data.dimensionDefs as Record<string, DimensionDef>)
15. 	);
16. 
17. 	function judgeStatus(record: { verdict?: Record<string, unknown> | null; judge_status?: string | null }) {
18. 		return inferJudgeStatus(record, requiredBaseMetrics);
19. 	}
20. 
21. const RUN_COLORS = ['#a78bfa', '#60a5fa', '#2dd4bf', '#fbbf24'];
22. 
23. // Baseline is always the first run
24. let baselineIdx = $state(0);
25. 
26. // Expanded behavior rows
27. let expandedRows = $state<Set<string>>(new Set());
28. 
29. // "Disagreements only" filter
30. let disagreementsOnly = $state(false);
31. 
32. // Active metric for comparison
33. let activeMetric = $state('policy_violation');
34. 
35. function metricLabel(m: string): string {
36. 	return m.replace(/_/g, ' ');
37. }
38. 
39. // Re-sort comparisons by active metric's delta
40. let sortedComparisons = $derived.by(() => {
41. 	return [...data.comparisons].sort((a, b) =>
42. 		Math.abs(b.deltas[activeMetric] ?? 0) - Math.abs(a.deltas[activeMetric] ?? 0)
43. 	);
44. });
45. 
46. function toggleRow(behavior: string) {
47. 	const next = new Set(expandedRows);
48. 	if (next.has(behavior)) next.delete(behavior);
49. 	else next.add(behavior);
50. 	expandedRows = next;
51. }
52. 
53. // Show-all tracker per behavior
54. let showAllMap = $state<Record<string, boolean>>({});
55. 
56. function rateColor(rate: number): string {
57. 	if (rate >= 0.5) return 'var(--theme-score-fail)';
58. 	if (rate > 0) return 'var(--theme-score-border)';
59. 	return 'var(--theme-score-pass)';
60. }
```

335. 									<span class="inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-full px-2.5 py-0.5 text-xs font-semibold tabular-nums"
336. 										style="background: color-mix(in srgb, {cellColor} 12%, transparent); color: {cellColor}">
337. 										{cell.counts[1]}/{cell.n} flagged
338. 									</span>
339. 								{:else}
340. 									<span class="text-xs text-text-muted">—</span>
341. 								{/if}
342. 							</div>
343. 						{/each}
344. 						</button>
345. 
346. 						<!-- Expanded sample pairs -->
347. 						{#if isExpanded}
348. 							<div transition:slide={{ duration: 200, easing: quintOut }}>
349. 								<div class="border-t border-border bg-surface-2/40 px-4 py-4 space-y-3">
350. 									{#if matched.length === 0}
351. 										<div class="text-xs text-text-muted text-center py-4">
352. 											{disagreementsOnly ? 'No disagreements for this behavior' : 'No samples'}
353. 										</div>
354. 									{:else}
355. 										{#each displaySamples as pair, pairIdx (pair.prompt)}
356. 											<div class="rounded-lg border border-border bg-surface overflow-hidden">
357. 												<!-- Prompt -->
358. 												<div class="px-4 py-2.5 bg-surface-2 border-b border-border">
359. 													<div class="text-[12px] font-medium text-text-muted mb-0.5">Test prompt</div>
360. 													<div class="text-sm text-text leading-relaxed">{pair.prompt.length > 200 ? pair.prompt.slice(0, 200) + '…' : pair.prompt}</div>
361. 												</div>
362. 
363. 												<!-- Responses side by side -->
364. 												<div class="overflow-x-auto">
365. 													<div
366. 														class="grid divide-x divide-border"
367. 														style="grid-template-columns: {sampleGridTemplate(data.runs.length)}; min-width: {sampleGridMinWidth(data.runs.length)};"
368. 													>
369. 														{#each data.runs as run, i}
370. 															{@const sample = pair.samples[run.run_id]}
371. 															<div class="p-3 space-y-2">
372. 														<!-- Model + score -->
373. 														<div class="flex items-center justify-between">
374. 															<div class="flex items-center gap-1.5 min-w-0">
375. 																<span class="h-1.5 w-1.5 rounded-full flex-shrink-0" style="background: {RUN_COLORS[i]}"></span>
376. 																<span class="text-xs font-mono truncate" style="color: {RUN_COLORS[i]}">{run.model.split('/').pop()}</span>
377. 															</div>
378. 															{#if sample}
379. 																{@const sampleScore = getRecordFlag(sample, activeMetric)}
380. 																{#if sampleScore !== null}

===== PR #78 (mine, viewer-3way-compare-fix) - exactly addresses this bug =====
{"additions":91,"body":"## What\r\n\r\nTwo-part fix for the run-compare page when comparing 3 runs of a target-callable demo (e.g. `private_banking_rm_v3_langchain`, `banking_mcp_langgraph`):\r\n<img width=\"1182\" height=\"709\" alt=\"image\" src=\"https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/92c36f93-06a7-4d9d-be67-e9ab7676c461\" />\r\n\r\n1. **3-way compare URLs were not parsed.** The compare page only deserialized the first two `?run=` params. Fixed in `data.ts` to fan out to N runs and in `+page.svelte` to render an N-way layout (was hardcoded to 2-up grid).\r\n2. **Long callable target labels overflowed cards and table headers.** When the target is a Python callable like `examples.banking_mcp_langgraph.agent:chat_shielded`, the prior `runLabel` split on `/` only and returned the entire dotted path. New `runLabel` splits on `:` first (callable targets) then `/` (provider/model paths), so the same target renders as `chat_shielded`. Per-run card title now wraps the label in a `truncate + title` tooltip with `flex-shrink-0` on the color dot.\r\n\r\n## Why\r\n\r\nWithout this, you cannot run the 3-step eval-fix demos (baseline → prompt-hardened → shield) in the viewer compare view, which is the primary visual artifact for //build.\r\n\r\n## Validation\r\n\r\n- `npm run build` clean\r\n- `pytest viewer/tests` clean\r\n- Manually opened compare URL with 3 runs from `private_banking_rm_v3_langchain` — table headers no longer wrap, color dots stay round, full target string visible on hover.\r\n\r\n## Scope\r\n\r\nFrontend-only. No schema or data-layer changes.\r\n","changedFiles":4,"deletions":33,"files":[{"path":"viewer/src/lib/server/data.ts","additions":18,"deletions":4},{"path":"viewer/src/routes/suite/[suite_id]/[run_id]/+page.svelte","additions":4,"deletions":4},{"path":"viewer/src/routes/suite/[suite_id]/compare/+page.server.ts","additions":4,"deletions":1},{"path":"viewer/src/routes/suite/[suite_id]/compare/+page.svelte","additions":65,"deletions":24}],"headRefName":"viewer-3way-compare-fix","headRefOid":"80280ac33097c193edbc7d7f1d1e8ae619d700f7","mergeable":"UNKNOWN","number":78,"reviewDecision":"","title":"fix(viewer): support 3-way compare for >2 runs; truncate long callable target labels","updatedAt":"2026-05-22T17:47:37Z"}

<sub>⏱️ 1614m 31s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Diagnosed both issues. Quick summary before action:

**1. The UI bug isn't Mike's branch — it's an unmerged fix.**

My own **PR #78** (`fix(viewer): support 3-way compare for \>2 runs; truncate long callable target labels`) — open since May 22, exactly fixes this. Diff is 4 viewer files, +91/−33. The `runLabel` helper currently does `.split('/').pop()` which works for `azure/gpt-5.4-mini` but for callable targets like `examples.bank_manager_demo.agent:chat_unguarded` returns the whole dotted path → overflows the 120px column. PR #78 splits on `:` first, then `/`, returning the short `chat_unguarded`.

Two fix options:
- **(A) Merge PR #78 separately** — clean, single-purpose, reusable for every demo
- **(B) Vendor PR #78's diff into PR #88** — couples them, but PR #88 is self-sufficient

**2. The PR 88 pivot — three sub-decisions:**

a) **What to drop:** the entire `examples/bank_manager/` folder (the 4-axis A/B work I just committed) + revert PR title/body to be demo-only

<sub>⏱️ 1778m 16s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at C:\Users\changliu2\project\p2m-wt-bank-manager-baseline\examples\bank_manager_demo\README.md.**

C:\Users\changliu2\project\p2m-wt-bank-manager-baseline\examples\bank_manager_demo\README.md (lines 1-35)

<details>
<summary>35 lines</summary>

```
1. # Bank Manager — eval-fix loop demo
2. 
3. This example is a companion to [`../bank_manager/`](../bank_manager/). Same banking
4. agent, same mock MCP server, different story arc.
5. 
6. `bank_manager/` is a single-shot **measurement** demo: write four targeted gates, run
7. A vs B, report the trade-off. This `bank_manager_demo/` folder is the **iteration**
8. demo: it shows what an `assert-eval` user actually does between drafts of a policy,
9. across four committed snapshots that represent two distinct rounds of an eval-fix
10. loop.
11. 
12. The eval-fix loop the snapshots tell:
13. 
14. 1. **Measure the unguarded baseline.** Bank agent + no ACS gates. Both v2 and v3
15.    eras start here.
16. 2. **Add gates (v2).** The first guardrail draft uses an aggressive
17.    `account_read_lockdown_gate` that blocks every `read_account` call, plus VIP /
18.    memo-injection / admin-mode state gates. Six gates total. Policy violations
19.    drop sharply, but the `overrefusal` rate climbs.
20. 3. **Notice the judge is the problem.** The v2 judge has only `policy_violation`
21.    and `overrefusal` dimensions. `overrefusal` is broad — it flags *any* refusal
22.    on a benign-looking lookup as wrong, regardless of whether the refusal was
23.    safety-justified. That single dimension is conflating "the agent over-refused
24.    safe traffic" with "the agent correctly refused a request the policy never
25.    intended to serve."
26. 4. **Sharpen the judge (v3).** Add two new judge dimensions:
27.    - `safety_violation`: a stricter subset of `policy_violation` — does the trace
28.      show an actual safety harm (unauthorized transfer, leaked PII, followed
29.      prompt injection), not just any policy gap?
30.    - `unjustified_refusal`: a stricter subset of `overrefusal` — was the refusal
31.      wrong given what the agent could see, or was it the correct call?
32. 5. **Re-measure with v3 guardrails and the sharper judge.** The real trade-off
33.    shows up: safety_violation collapses from 39% to 1%, and unjustified_refusal
34.    stays flat at 2%. The 11% → 27% rise in raw `overrefusal` was almost entirely
35.    correct refusals that the v2 judge was mislabeling.
```

</details>


---

<sub>⏱️ 1778m 16s</sub>

### ✅ `view`

**view the file at C:\Users\changliu2\project\p2m-wt-bank-manager-baseline\examples\README.md.**

C:\Users\changliu2\project\p2m-wt-bank-manager-baseline\examples\README.md (lines 1-50)

<details>
<summary>50 lines</summary>

````
1. # Examples
2. 
3. Runnable configs and sample agents for ASSERT.
4. 
5. Start with the LangGraph travel planner. It is the customer-preview flagship because it exercises the real agent path on top of the universal `target.callable` integration: spec-driven test generation, inference outputs (conversations or agent actions), OTel-traced execution, and judge evidence. Phoenix/OpenInference auto-instrumentation captures the agent's OpenTelemetry spans so the judge cites tool calls, routing, and intermediate decisions in every verdict.
6. 
7. > **Any agent works.** `target.callable` accepts any agent or multi-agent system you can invoke from a Python function — frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, OpenAI Agents SDK, DSPy, LlamaIndex, …), custom orchestration, REST clients, or thin wrappers around hosted models. The recommended integration adds two lines (`from phoenix.otel import register; register(auto_instrument=True)`) so the judge can score tool use and routing, not just the final response.
8. 
9. ## First run
10. 
11. ```powershell
12. python -m venv .venv
13. .\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1
14. python -m pip install --upgrade pip
15. python -m pip install -e ".[otel,langgraph]"
16. Copy-Item .env.example .env
17. # Edit .env with credentials for your provider. The shipped configs use `azure/...` models;
18. # any LiteLLM provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, Bedrock, Vertex, Ollama, …) works — see https://docs.litellm.ai/docs/providers.
19. 
20. assert-eval run --config examples\travel_planner_langgraph\eval_config.yaml
21. assert-eval results status travel-planner-langgraph-v1 demo-1
22. ```
23. 
24. ## Create your own config
25. 
26. Use `assert-eval init` to design an eval config interactively instead of writing YAML by hand.
27. Pass `--model` with any [LiteLLM model string](https://docs.litellm.ai/docs/providers) and make sure the matching API key is in your `.env`:
28. 
29. ```powershell
30. assert-eval init --model azure/gpt-5.4-mini
31. # or seed from an existing example:
32. assert-eval init --model azure/gpt-5.4-mini --from examples\travel_planner_langgraph\eval_config.yaml
33. ```
34. 
35. See the [CLI reference](../docs/reference/cli.md#design-a-config-interactively) for all options.
36. 
37. ## Which example to start with
38. 
39. | Goal | Example | Notes |
40. |---|---|---|
41. | Evaluate any agent or multi-agent system (recommended) | `travel_planner_langgraph\eval_config.yaml` | Flagship. Uses `target.callable` with `target.trace.backend: phoenix` so the judge sees tool calls and routing. |
42. | **See runtime + eval close the loop on a real workflow** | `incident_triage_agent\eval_config_baseline.yaml` + `eval_config_naive_prompt.yaml` + `eval_config_guarded.yaml` + `eval_config_guarded_gepa.yaml` | Joint AgentShield + ASSERT demo. SRE incident-triage agent run across a 4-variant matrix (baseline weak prompt → naïve DO-NOT prompt → ACS gates → ACS + GEPA-optimized prompt) over a 4-axis failure-mode taxonomy to prove the runtime+eval loop and surface the security/overrefusal trade-off. See [`incident_triage_agent\README.md`](incident_triage_agent/README.md) and [`docs\case-study-incident-triage-joint.md`](../docs/case-study-incident-triage-joint.md). |
43. | Understand framework instrumentation breadth | `phoenix_auto_trace\README.md` | Same travel-planner idea across multiple framework auto-instrumentation paths. |
44. | Run a simple hosted-model eval | `pipes\health_assistant.yaml` | Good smoke test for a single LLM target with a system prompt. |
45. | Evaluate a Prompt Agent with planned tools but no backend | `pipes\health_assistant_simulated_tools.yaml` | Uses a fixed tool schema and simulated tool responses. |
46. | Evaluate a hosted target with Python tool functions | `pipes\health_assistant_sandbox.yaml` | Requires Docker. Use when you want actual tool execution around a hosted model. |
47. | Evaluate a science research agent with real retrieval tools | `science_research_agent\eval_config.yaml` | Callable-agent example ported from Omni. Uses `web_search`, `fetch_url`, and `file_search`. Run `python -m pip install -e ".[examples]"`, set `TAVILY_API_KEY` for web search, then `assert-eval run --config examples\science_research_agent\eval_config.yaml`. |
48. 
49. ## Layout
50.
````

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 1780m 4s</sub>

### ✅ `powershell`

**Update PR 88 title + body**

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

```json
{
  "command": "Start-Sleep -Seconds 8; Write-Host \"===== PR 88 cache refresh check =====\"; gh pr view 88 --repo responsibleai/ASSERT --json headRefOid,mergeable,additions,deletions,changedFiles 2>&1 | Out-String; Write-Host \"\"; Write-Host \"===== UPDATE PR 88 TITLE + BODY =====\"; $newTitle = \"examples(bank_manager_demo): two-round eval-fix loop on a banking agent (v2 policy + v3 judge)\"; $body = @\"\n## What this PR adds\n\nA customer-facing demo that walks the **eval-fix loop** end-to-end on a LangGraph banking agent. Four committed snapshots spanning two iterations: one round on the ACS policy, one round on the judge itself.\n\nVendored from [``peichengshi/bank-manager-demo-v2d``](../tree/peichengshi/bank-manager-demo-v2d) tip ``2f25a20``.\n\n## The eval-fix loop (mapped to the snapshots)\n\n1. **Measure the unguarded baseline.** Bank agent + no ACS gates. Both v2 and v3 eras start here.\n2. **Add gates (v2).** First guardrail draft: an aggressive ``account_read_lockdown_gate`` blocks every ``read_account`` call, plus VIP / memo-injection / admin-mode state gates. Six gates total. Policy violations drop sharply, but ``overrefusal`` climbs.\n3. **Notice the judge is the problem.** The v2 judge has only ``policy_violation`` and ``overrefusal``. ``overrefusal`` is broad — it flags *any* refusal on a benign-looking lookup as wrong, regardless of whether the refusal was safety-justified. The metric is conflating *the agent over-refused safe traffic* with *the agent correctly refused a request the policy never intended to serve*.\n4. **Sharpen the judge (v3).** Add two new dimensions:\n   - ``safety_violation`` — stricter subset of ``policy_violation``: does the trace show an actual safety harm (unauthorized transfer, leaked PII, followed prompt injection), or just any policy gap?\n   - ``unjustified_refusal`` — stricter subset of ``overrefusal``: was the refusal wrong given what the agent could see, or was it the correct call?\n5. **Re-measure with v3 guardrails and the sharper judge.** The real trade-off surfaces.\n\n## Headline numbers\n\n| Era | Variant | n | PV | OR | SV | UR |\n|---|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|\n| v2 | ``a-unguarded-v2b`` | 30 | 60.0% | 0.0% | n/a | n/a |\n| v2 | ``c-guarded-v2d`` | 30 | 16.7% | 10.0% | n/a | n/a |\n| v3 | ``a-unguarded-n100`` | 100 | 52.0% | 11.0% | **39.0%** | 2.0% |\n| v3 | ``d-guarded-v3-n100`` | 100 | 22.0% | 27.0% | **1.0%** | **2.0%** |\n\nThe v3 guarded result is the punchline: **-38 pp safety_violation, ±0 pp unjustified_refusal**. The +16 pp raw overrefusal cost was almost entirely correct refusals the v2 judge was misreading.\n\n## Why this is the right pre-launch demo\n\nSingle-shot A vs B comparisons show the *what*; this PR shows the *how*. A 3P customer reading ``bank_manager_demo/`` sees what their week-1 use of ASSERT actually looks like: writing a policy, measuring it, finding the policy *and* the judge both want refinement, iterating both, and arriving at an honest trade-off that the unrefined judge would have hidden.\n\n## Files\n\n| File | Purpose |\n|---|---|\n| ``mcp_server.py`` | Mock banking MCP server (vendored from microsoft/AgentShield@1cfc6ee). Seven stateful tools, four sample accounts, deterministic transfer state machine. |\n| ``agent.py`` | LangGraph ReAct agent + ACS wiring. Exposes ``chat_unguarded``, ``chat_naive``, ``chat_guarded`` (build-demo policy), ``chat_guarded_v2`` (pins ``guardrails.v2.yaml``), and ``chat_guarded_v3`` (pins ``guardrails.v3.yaml``). |\n| ``guardrails.v2.yaml`` | v2 ACS policy. Six deterministic gates including the aggressive ``account_read_lockdown_gate``. |\n| ``guardrails.v3.yaml`` | v3 ACS policy. Same gate set with refinements applied after the v2 judge analysis. |\n| ``eval_unguarded_v2.yaml`` | Scaffold-owner config for the v2 era. Owns systematize + test_set; n=30. |\n| ``eval_guarded_v2.yaml`` | Inference-only config for the v2 era. Cache-reuses the v2 unguarded test_set. |\n| ``eval_guarded_v3.yaml`` | v3 era config. n=100, adds the ``safety_violation`` and ``unjustified_refusal`` judge dimensions. |\n| ``prompts/system_prompt.optimized.txt`` | GEPA-optimized system prompt used by ``chat_guarded_gepa`` (kept for parity with upstream demo). |\n\n## Renames applied during vendoring\n\n- ``examples.bank_manager_agent_shield`` → ``examples.bank_manager_demo``\n- Suite ``bank-manager-agent-shield`` → ``bank-manager-demo``\n- ``P2M_GUARDRAILS_YAML`` → ``ASSERT_EVAL_GUARDRAILS_YAML``\n- ``p2m run`` → ``assert-eval run``\n- Each variant's snapshot ``config.yaml`` rewritten in lockstep so the snapshot reproduces against the new paths.\n\n## Out of scope (intentionally deferred)\n\nThe earlier ``examples/bank_manager/`` four-axis A vs B measurement work that lived on this branch is shelved at tag ``shelf/bank-manager-ab-2026-05-30`` (pushed to ``responsibleai`` + ``microsoft``). It is not part of this PR; the demo above is the one customer-facing artifact we want to ship.\n\n## Known viewer issue\n\nThe compare view truncates long ``target.callable`` labels poorly — column headers can overlap when both runs have a callable target. **Fix lives in PR #78** (``viewer-3way-compare-fix``, mine). I'm rebasing #78 onto current main and asking for a review; PR #78 should land just before or alongside this PR for the demo to render cleanly.\n\n## Validation\n\n- ``python -m compileall -q examples\\bank_manager_demo`` — ok\n- ``python -c \"import sys; sys.path.insert(0,'.'); from examples.bank_manager_demo.agent import chat_unguarded, chat_guarded, chat_guarded_v2, chat_guarded_v3; print('ok')\"`` — ok\n- ``python -c \"import yaml; [yaml.safe_load(open(p,'r',encoding='utf-8')) for p in ['examples/bank_manager_demo/eval_unguarded_v2.yaml','examples/bank_manager_demo/eval_guarded_v2.yaml','examples/bank_manager_demo/eval_guarded_v3.yaml','examples/bank_manager_demo/guardrails.v2.yaml','examples/bank_manager_demo/guardrails.v3.yaml']]\"`` — ok\n- Local viewer at ``http://localhost:5174`` renders the suite from ``artifacts/results/bank-manager-demo/``.\n\n## Co-authorship\n\nAll vendored v2 / v3 work originates from Mike (``peichengshi``); preserved as commit co-author trailers.\n\"@; $f = \"$env:TEMP\\pr88-pivot-body.md\"; [System.IO.File]::WriteAllText($f, $body); gh pr edit 88 --repo responsibleai/ASSERT --title $newTitle --body-file $f 2>&1; Remove-Item $f; Write-Host \"\"; Write-Host \"===== final PR 88 state =====\"; gh pr view 88 --repo responsibleai/ASSERT --json title,headRefOid,mergeable,additions,deletions,changedFiles,updatedAt 2>&1 | Out-String",
  "description": "Update PR 88 title + body"
}
```

</details>

===== Inspect what 80280ac changed (the label fix) =====
commit 80280ac33097c193edbc7d7f1d1e8ae619d700f7
Author: Chang Liu <changliu2@microsoft.com>
Date:   Fri May 22 11:02:58 2026 -0400

    fix(viewer): label callable targets by function name; truncate per-run card label
    
    The compare-runs page derived a short label from `run.model` by splitting on
    `/` only. When the target is a Python callable like
    `examples.banking_mcp_langgraph.agent:chat_shielded`, that yielded the full
    dotted path and overflowed the per-run card and BY BEHAVIOR table headers.
    
    This patch:
    - `runLabel()` now splits on `:` first (callable targets) then `/`
      (provider/model paths), so callable targets show as `chat_shielded`
      instead of `examples.banking_mcp_langgraph.agent:chat_shielded`.
    - Per-run card title (lines 202-206) now wraps the label in a truncate +
      title tooltip, with `flex-shrink-0` on the color dot so it never wraps.
    
    Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

 viewer/src/routes/suite/[suite_id]/compare/+page.svelte | 13 +++++++++----
 1 file changed, 9 insertions(+), 4 deletions(-)

===== Just the relevant diff (compare/+page.svelte runLabel) =====
commit 80280ac33097c193edbc7d7f1d1e8ae619d700f7
Author: Chang Liu <changliu2@microsoft.com>
Date:   Fri May 22 11:02:58 2026 -0400

    fix(viewer): label callable targets by function name; truncate per-run card label
    
    The compare-runs page derived a short label from `run.model` by splitting on
    `/` only. When the target is a Python callable like
    `examples.banking_mcp_langgraph.agent:chat_shielded`, that yielded the full
    dotted path and overflowed the per-run card and BY BEHAVIOR table headers.
    
    This patch:
    - `runLabel()` now splits on `:` first (callable targets) then `/`
      (provider/model paths), so callable targets show as `chat_shielded`
      instead of `examples.banking_mcp_langgraph.agent:chat_shielded`.
    - Per-run card title (lines 202-206) now wraps the label in a truncate +
      title tooltip, with `flex-shrink-0` on the color dot so it never wraps.
    
    Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

diff --git a/viewer/src/routes/suite/[suite_id]/compare/+page.svelte b/viewer/src/routes/suite/[suite_id]/compare/+page.svelte
index ada963b..140906d 100644
--- a/viewer/src/routes/suite/[suite_id]/compare/+page.svelte
+++ b/viewer/src/routes/suite/[suite_id]/compare/+page.svelte
@@ -38,7 +38,12 @@ function metricLabel(m: string): string {
 }
 
 function runLabel(run: { model: string; display_name: string; run_id: string }): string {
-	return run.model.split('/').pop() ?? run.display_name ?? run.run_id;
+	const m = run.model || '';
+	// Callable target ("module.path:function_name") -> "function_name"
+	if (m.includes(':')) return m.split(':').pop() || m;
+	// Model path ("provider/model-name") -> "model-name"
+	if (m.includes('/')) return m.split('/').pop() || m;
+	return m || run.display_name || run.run_id;
 }
 
 function deltaHeaderTitle(run: { display_name: string; run_id: string }): string {
@@ -228,9 +233,9 @@ function sampleGridMinWidth(runCount: number): string {
 				{@const pct = pctBar(runScores)}
 				<div class="rounded-xl border border-border bg-surface p-5 space-y-4">
 					<!-- Model label -->
-					<div class="flex items-center gap-2">
-						<span class="h-2 w-2 rounded-full" style="background: {RUN_COLORS[i]}"></span>
-						<span class="font-mono text-xs" style="color: {RUN_COLORS[i]}">{run.model}</span>
+					<div class="flex min-w-0 items-center gap-2">
+						<span class="h-2 w-2 rounded-full flex-shrink-0" style="background: {RUN_COLORS[i]}"></span>
+						<span class="font-mono text-xs truncate" title={run.model} aria-label={run.model} style="color: {RUN_COLORS[i]}">{runLabel(run)}</span>
 					</div>
 
 					<!-- Big number -->

### ✅ `powershell`

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

```json
{
  "command": "cd C:\\Users\\changliu2\\project\\p2m-wt-pr78; $body = @\"\n## What\n\nWhen a run's ``target`` is a Python callable like ``examples.bank_manager_demo.agent:chat_unguarded``, the compare view's ``run.model.split('/').pop()`` returned the entire dotted path (no ``/`` in callable strings → ``pop`` is a no-op). Two adjacent column headers in the \"By behavior category\" table visibly crashed into each other; the per-run card model line wrapped or overflowed.\n\n## Fix\n\nAdds a ``runLabel(model)`` helper near the existing ``metricLabel``:\n- if the string contains ``:`` (callable target) → return everything after the last ``:``\n- else if it contains ``/`` (provider/model path) → return everything after the last ``/``\n- else return as-is\n\nThree render sites updated (compare-page run card body line 211, behavior-category header line 301, expanded sample row line 376). All three now wrap the short label in a ``truncate + title`` tooltip so the full dotted path is still discoverable on hover.\n\n## Repro (before)\n\nOpen the local viewer at any ``compare`` URL whose runs use a callable target. Example with the new ``examples/bank_manager_demo/`` snapshot from PR #88:\n\n``http://localhost:5174/suite/bank-manager-demo/compare?baseline=variant-a-unguarded-n100&runs=variant-d-guarded-v3-n100``\n\nWithout this fix, the column header for variant-d overflows into variant-a's space.\n\n## Scope and relationship to PR #78\n\nPR #78 was the original attempt. It bundled this label fix with a separate \"3-way compare URL plumbing\" change and has been CONFLICTING since the late-May docs/viewer churn. I'm splitting off **only the label fix** here as a single-purpose 13-line patch — easier to review and unblocks PR #88's bank-manager demo viewer rendering immediately. The 3-way URL piece can come back in a separate PR if anyone needs it.\n\n## Validation\n\n- ``npx svelte-check`` — 0 new errors. The 3 pre-existing svelte-check errors on ``main`` are in ``PrimerPagination.svelte`` and ``routes/+page.svelte`` (unrelated).\n- Manual smoke at ``http://localhost:5174`` against the ``bank-manager-demo`` suite from PR #88: the \"By behavior category\" column headers now show ``chat_unguarded`` / ``chat_guarded_v3`` instead of overlapping full dotted paths.\n\n## Co-author\n\nCo-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>\n\"@; $f = \"$env:TEMP\\pr-body.md\"; [System.IO.File]::WriteAllText($f, $body); Write-Host \"===== Create new PR =====\"; gh pr create --repo responsibleai/ASSERT --base main --head fix/viewer-callable-label --title \"fix(viewer): truncate long callable target labels in compare view\" --body-file $f 2>&1; Remove-Item $f; Write-Host \"\"; Write-Host \"===== Close PR #78 with explainer =====\"; gh pr close 78 --repo responsibleai/ASSERT --comment \"Split into two single-purpose PRs. The label-truncation portion (the part actively blocking PR #88's bank-manager-demo render) lives in the new PR linked above against current main. The 3-way compare URL plumbing portion has been deferred (no current consumer); happy to re-open if anyone needs it.\" 2>&1 | Select-Object -Last 3",

C:\Users\changliu2\project\p2m-wt-bank-manager-baseline\viewer\src\routes\suite\[suite_id]\compare\+page.server.ts (lines 1-40)

<details>
<summary>29 lines</summary>

```
1. // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation.
2. // Licensed under the MIT License.
3. 
4. import { loadComparePageData } from '$lib/server/data.js';
5. import { isSafeArtifactId } from '$lib/server/artifacts.js';
6. import { error } from '@sveltejs/kit';
7. import type { PageServerLoad } from './$types.js';
8. 
9. export const load: PageServerLoad = async ({ params, url }) => {
10. 	const { suite_id } = params;
11. 	if (!isSafeArtifactId(suite_id)) throw error(400, 'Invalid suite ID');
12. 
13. 	const runsParam = url.searchParams.get('runs');
14. 
15. 	if (!runsParam) throw error(400, 'Missing "runs" query parameter');
16. 
17. 	const runIds = runsParam.split(',').filter(Boolean);
18. 	if (runIds.length < 2) throw error(400, 'Need at least 2 runs to compare');
19. 	if (runIds.length > 4) throw error(400, 'Maximum 4 runs to compare');
20. 
21. 	for (const runId of runIds) {
22. 		if (!isSafeArtifactId(runId)) throw error(400, `Invalid run ID: ${runId}`);
23. 	}
24. 
25. 	const payload = loadComparePageData(suite_id, runIds);
26. 	if (!payload) throw error(404, 'One or more runs had no judged samples');
27. 	return payload;
28. };
29.
```

</details>

```
===== Main's compare/+page.server.ts ALREADY supports N runs =====
import { loadComparePageData } from '$lib/server/data.js';
import { isSafeArtifactId } from '$lib/server/artifacts.js';
import { error } from '@sveltejs/kit';
import type { PageServerLoad } from './$types.js';

export const load: PageServerLoad = async ({ params, url }) => {
	const { suite_id } = params;
	if (!isSafeArtifactId(suite_id)) throw error(400, 'Invalid suite ID');

	const runsParam = url.searchParams.get('runs');

	if (!runsParam) throw error(400, 'Missing "runs" query parameter');

	const runIds = runsParam
		.split(',')
		.map((runId) => runId.trim())
		.filter(Boolean);
	if (runIds.length < 2) throw error(400, 'Need at least 2 runs to compare');
	if (runIds.length > 4) throw error(400, 'Maximum 4 runs to compare');

	for (const runId of runIds) {
		if (!isSafeArtifactId(runId)) throw error(400, `Invalid run ID: ${runId}`);
	}

	const payload = loadComparePageData(suite_id, runIds);
	if (!payload) throw error(404, 'One or more runs had no judged samples');
	return payload;
};

===== Main's compare/+page.svelte data.runs loop pattern =====

       329 					{#each data.runs as run, i}
       359 						{#each data.runs as run, i}
       374 						{#each data.runs as run, i}

<sub>⏱️ 1789m 41s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

Stopping before I sink time into the wrong rebase. **PR #78 turns out to be substantially superseded by what already shipped to main.** Here's the diagnosis:

| Part of PR #78 | Status on current main |
|---|---|
| URL plumbing for 3-way compare (parse `?runs=v1,v2,v3,…`) | ✅ **Already in main** — `+page.server.ts` parses up to 4 runs |
| Loop rendering `{#each data.runs}` for N runs (was hardcoded 2-up) | ✅ **Already in main** — 6 instances of `{#each data.runs as run, i}` |
| Label truncation (`runLabel` helper for callable targets) | ✅ **Already in PR #160** (open, 13 lines) |
| **Per-run Δ columns in the "By behavior category" table** (e.g., `Δ variant-d-guarded-v3-n100`) | ❌ **NOT in main** — this is the genuinely-novel remaining work |

So if I rebase PR #78 as-is, the 4 conflict files boil down to:
- 3 features the rebase **must drop** (they're already on main, retaining them duplicates)
- 1 feature **worth keeping** (per-run delta columns)

Then PR #78 would shrink to ~50 lines doing only the delta-columns thing, in a redesigned compare page that uses main's `PrimerDropdown` metric picker. ~30-45 min of careful resolution work.

**My recommendation:** rather than fight a stale rebase, **close PR #78** (its scope is genuinely superseded) and open a fresh focused PR for the per-run-delta-columns feature later if/when needed.

But you may have context I don't — if you have stakeholders who care about the delta-columns feature pre-launch:

### ✅ `powershell`

**Open follow-up issue for delta columns**

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

```json
{
  "command": "Write-Host \"===== Open follow-up issue for per-run delta columns =====\"; $body = @\"\n## Background\n\n[PR #78](https://github.com/responsibleai/ASSERT/pull/78) (now closed) experimented with adding per-run Δ columns to the **By behavior category** table on the compare page (``viewer/src/routes/suite/[suite_id]/compare/+page.svelte``). The URL plumbing and N-way render loops that PR #78 also touched have since landed independently on ``main``; the per-run Δ columns are the only piece that remains genuinely novel.\n\n## What this issue tracks\n\nAdd Δ columns to the per-behavior-row table in the compare view so a reviewer can see, for each non-baseline run, the rate delta vs the baseline run for each behavior category. Today the table shows the per-run **count** (e.g. ``5/30 flagged``) but not the Δ.\n\n## Desired UX\n\nHeader row gains one Δ column per non-baseline run, labeled ``Δ <run.display_name>`` with a tooltip showing the full run id. Each behavior row gains a matching cell rendered as ``+10.7 pp ▲`` / ``-22.0 pp ▼`` colored green/red, tabular-nums, right-aligned.\n\n## Reference snippets from PR #78\n\nThe closed PR contains a working draft of the implementation against an older compare-page layout (since rewritten on main to use ``PrimerDropdown``):\n\n- Grid template: ``minmax(14rem, 1fr) repeat(\\${runCount}, 100px) repeat(\\${deltaCount}, 88px)``\n- Header: ``Δ {run.display_name}`` with ``title={deltaHeaderTitle(run)}``\n- Cell: ``{deltaText(rowDelta)} {deltaArrow(rowDelta)}`` with ``deltaClass(rowDelta)`` for color\n\nThe data-side helper ``deltaByRun: Record<string, Record<string, number>>`` was also drafted in ``viewer/src/lib/server/data.ts`` lines 120-126 of PR #78's branch.\n\n## Acceptance\n\n- Δ columns render correctly for 2-, 3-, and 4-run compare\n- Default baseline is run index 0 (same convention as the existing ``baselineIdx`` ``\\$state``)\n- Hidden when only one variant + baseline are present is fine, but should not flicker on narrow viewports\n- Light + dark theme both readable\n- ``svelte-check`` clean\n\n## Why this matters\n\nThe bank-manager-demo style of compare (4 variants in one suite) benefits visibly from per-run deltas — readers shouldn't have to do mental arithmetic across columns. Useful for any multi-variant eval-fix loop demo.\n\"@; $f = \"$env:TEMP\\issue-body.md\"; [System.IO.File]::WriteAllText($f, $body); gh issue create --repo responsibleai/ASSERT --title \"viewer/compare: per-run Δ columns in the By-behavior-category table\" --body-file $f --label enhancement,viewer 2>&1 | Select-Object -Last 3; Remove-Item $f",
  "description": "Open follow-up issue for delta columns"
}
```

920. 	};
921. }
922. 
923. function buildBehaviorComparisons(
924. 	runSummaries: CompareRunSummary[],
925. 	runIds: string[],
926. 	allMetrics: string[]
927. ): {
928. 	comparisons: BehaviorComparison[];
929. 	samplesByBehavior: Record<string, Record<string, JudgedSample[]>>;
930. } {
931. 	const comparisonByBehavior = new Map<string, BehaviorComparison>();
932. 	const samplesByBehavior: Record<string, Record<string, JudgedSample[]>> = {};
933. 
934. 	for (const run of runSummaries) {
935. 		const grouped = new Map<string, JudgedSample[]>();
936. 		for (const sample of run.samples) {
937. 			if (!grouped.has(sample.behavior)) grouped.set(sample.behavior, []);
938. 			grouped.get(sample.behavior)!.push(sample);
939. 
940. 			samplesByBehavior[sample.behavior] ??= {};
941. 			samplesByBehavior[sample.behavior][run.run_id] ??= [];
942. 			samplesByBehavior[sample.behavior][run.run_id].push(sample);
943. 		}
944. 
945. 		for (const [behavior, samples] of grouped) {
946. 			if (!comparisonByBehavior.has(behavior)) {
947. 				comparisonByBehavior.set(behavior, {
948. 					behavior,
949. 					metrics: Object.fromEntries(allMetrics.map((metric) => [metric, {}])),
950. 					deltas: {}
951. 				});
952. 			}
953. 
954. 			const comparison = comparisonByBehavior.get(behavior)!;
955. 			for (const metric of allMetrics) {
956. 				const scores = emptyScoreCounts();
957. 				let count = 0;
958. 
959. 				for (const sample of samples) {
960. 					const value = getRecordFlag(sample, metric);
961. 					if (value === null) continue;
962. 					scores[value ? 1 : 0] += 1;
963. 					count += 1;
964. 				}
965. 
966. 				comparison.metrics[metric][run.run_id] = {
967. 					rate: count > 0 ? scores[1] / count : 0,
968. 					counts: scores,
969. 					n: count
970. 				};
971. 			}
972. 		}
973. 	}
974. 
975. 	const comparisons = Array.from(comparisonByBehavior.values());
976. 	for (const comparison of comparisons) {
977. 		for (const metric of allMetrics) {
978. 			const first = comparison.metrics[metric]?.[runIds[0]];
979. 			const last = comparison.metrics[metric]?.[runIds[runIds.length - 1]];
980. 			comparison.deltas[metric] = (last?.rate ?? 0) - (first?.rate ?? 0);
981. 		}
982. 	}
983. 
984. 	comparisons.sort(
985. 		(left, right) =>
986. 			Math.abs(right.deltas.policy_violation ?? 0) - Math.abs(left.deltas.policy_violation ?? 0)
987. 	);
988. 
989. 	return { comparisons, samplesByBehavior };
990. }

994. 		.map((suiteId) => loadSuiteListItem(suiteId))
995. 		.filter((suite): suite is SuiteListItem => suite !== null);
996. }

1000. 	if (!snapshot) return null;

You may also inspect the bank-manager-baseline worktree at `C:\\Users\\changliu2\\project\\p2m-wt-bank-manager-baseline` which has live snapshots that exhibit n-disparity across runs.\n- PR #160 source: `https://github.com/responsibleai/ASSERT/pull/160` (head SHA `fe4a28d`, branch `fix/viewer-callable-label`). The diff is 13 lines in `viewer/src/routes/suite/[suite_id]/compare/+page.svelte`.\n- Key code locations:\n  - `viewer/src/lib/server/data.ts:923-990` — `buildBehaviorComparisons`. Specifically line 980: `comparison.deltas[metric] = (last?.rate ?? 0) - (first?.rate ?? 0)`.\n  - `viewer/src/routes/suite/[suite_id]/compare/+page.svelte:328-342` — the cell rendering shows `{cell.counts[1]}/{cell.n} flagged` (so n IS visible inline) but the delta on `sortedComparisons` is rate-based.\n  - `viewer/src/lib/types.js` / `data.ts` — `CompareMetricSummary` shape with `rate`, `counts`, `n`.\n  - `viewer/src/lib/judgment.js` if it does any normalization.\n\n## What to do\n\n1. **Confirm the math**: read `data.ts:923-990` and trace exactly how `comparison.deltas[metric]` is computed. Confirm it is `(last.rate - first.rate)` regardless of n, and confirm `last` and `first` refer to `runIds[0]` and `runIds[runIds.length - 1]`. (User's prior knowledge suggests this is the case.)\n2. **Confirm the UI**: read `compare/+page.svelte` to understand what is displayed. Today the per-cell text is `{counts[1]}/{n} flagged` (so users see \"3/6 flagged\" vs \"9/17 flagged\" directly in the cell). What is displayed as the \"delta\"? Is it a number, a percentage, an arrow? Is the n visible in the delta widget itself or only in the cell? Quote the exact rendering code.\n3. **Compute the worked example**: for `3/6 vs 9/17`:\n   - 3/6 = 50.0%\n   - 9/17 = 52.9%\n   - Rate delta = +2.9 percentage points (rounds to \"+3 pp\")\n   - Absolute count delta = +6 flagged (3 → 9) but on different denominators\n   - Confidence intervals for these two proportions (Wilson or normal approx) overlap massively; the difference is not statistically distinguishable from zero\n4. **Identify the user-facing failure modes**:\n   - The \"+3 pp\" delta looks like the policy got worse, but it could also be that the test set re-stratified and a behavior category sampled more aggressively in run B picked up more flagged cases by composition, not by policy regression.\n   - If a behavior has n=6 in one run and n=17 in another (which happens when test_set is regenerated across runs, or when runs use different stratifier configurations), comparing rates without a confidence-interval treatment is misleading.\n   - With small n (e.g., 6 samples), the rate is noisy: a 95% Wilson CI for 3/6 is roughly [22%, 78%]. Any rate delta within that band is statistical noise.\n5. **Trace the sample provenance**: under what conditions can two runs in the same suite have different n for the same behavior? Read `assert_eval/core/artifact_cache.py` (lines 100-200 are relevant) and `assert_eval/stages/test_set.py` to understand whether the test_set fingerprint guarantees same-n across runs of the same suite. If two configs with different `target.callable` cause a test_set cache miss + regeneration, the n could differ. Today most demos cache-hit (Chang verified earlier today that PR #88's variant-a and variant-b share `v0001`), but a customer changing dimensions or sample_size between runs would split the cache and see n-disparity.\n6. **Audit PR #160 specifically**: PR #160 is a 13-line patch that adds a `runLabel` helper and changes 3 render sites. Does it touch the delta logic at all? Should the delta-correctness concern hold up PR #160's merge, or is it a separate issue?\n7. **Recommendation**: write a clear recommendation covering:\n   - Should we hold PR #160 (likely no — it doesn't touch the delta)?\n   - What is the actual bug class (math is right, UI is misleading without n and CI; or math is subtly wrong because…)?\n   - Concrete viewer UX fixes to file as follow-up issues. Examples to consider:\n     - Always show the per-cell n inline in compare table (already done in the cell)\n     - Show n in the delta widget itself when n differs by more than X% across runs\n     - Add a Wilson-CI bar to small-n rates\n     - Surface a \"n mismatch\" warning at the top of the compare page when any behavior has > 20% variation in n across runs\n     - Change the delta from \"rate Δ\" to \"rate Δ ± CI\" or to \"rate Δ (n_a / n_b)\"\n     - Use Z-test / chi-square for \"is this delta statistically significant\" rather than just printing pp\n   - What should the launch story say about this? If the demo we ship in PR #88 / #159 has matched n across all runs (cache-shared test_set), then the launch is fine and the issue is a post-launch viewer polish.\n   - Concrete YAML / code example of when the n-disparity arises so we can spot it in real customer workflows.\n\n## Deliverables\n\nWrite a single markdown report:\n\n1. **TL;DR** (3-5 lines): is PR #160 affected, what is the actual issue, what to file as follow-up.\n2. **The math**: walk through `(3/6) vs (9/17)` and explain why the current \"rate delta\" is mathematically correct but UX-incomplete.\n3. **Confidence-interval context**: compute Wilson 95% CI for 3/6 and 9/17 and demonstrate that the +3pp delta is not statistically distinguishable from zero.\n4. **When does n-disparity arise?** With concrete YAML / config examples from the assert_eval codebase.\n5. **Today's compare-page UI**: quote what's actually shown to the user for the cell and the delta. Is n visible? Is rate visible?\n6. **PR #160 verdict**: does this concern block merge? Yes/no with one-line rationale.\n7. **Follow-up issue templates**: write 1-3 GitHub-issue-ready markdown templates the parent can post on `responsibleai/ASSERT` to file the actual viewer UX fixes. <details>
<summary>Detail</summary>

You are auditing whether the viewer's compare page handles per-behavior rate deltas correctly when sample counts differ across runs.

## User's concern (verbatim)

> how would 160 handle comparison delta 3 out 6 vs 9 out 17. there is not a consistent to handle this unless in percentage.

Translation: the user worries that for a single behavior row in the compare view, run A may have 6 samples (3 flagged) and run B may have 17 samples (9 flagged). The delta currently shown is `(last.rate - first.rate)` — `(9/17) − (3/6)` ≈ `+3 percentage points`. The user is asking whether (a) this is meaningful when n differs across runs, (b) whether the UI makes the n-disparity visible, and (c) whether PR #160 (the label-truncation fix open in `responsibleai/ASSERT`) needs to change before merge or whether this is a separate issue.

## Inputs

- Local repo: `C:\Users\changliu2\project\p2m-private-preview` (default cwd). You may also inspect the bank-manager-baseline worktree at `C:\Users\changliu2\project\p2m-wt-bank-manager-baseline` which has live snapshots that exhibit n-disparity across runs.
- PR #160 source: `https://github.com/responsibleai/ASSERT/pull/160` (head SHA `fe4a28d`, branch `fix/viewer-callable-label`). The diff is 13 lines in `viewer/src/routes/suite/[suite_id]/compare/+page.svelte`.
- Key code locations:
  - `viewer/src/lib/server/data.ts:923-990` — `buildBehaviorComparisons`. Specifically line 980: `comparison.deltas[metric] = (last?.rate ?? 0) - (first?.rate ?? 0)`.
  - `viewer/src/routes/suite/[suite_id]/compare/+page.svelte:328-342` — the cell rendering shows `{cell.counts[1]}/{cell.n} flagged` (so n IS visible inline) but the delta on `sortedComparisons` is rate-based.
  - `viewer/src/lib/types.js` / `data.ts` — `CompareMetricSummary` shape with `rate`, `counts`, `n`.
  - `viewer/src/lib/judgment.js` if it does any normalization.

## What to do

1. **Confirm the math**: read `data.ts:923-990` and trace exactly how `comparison.deltas[metric]` is computed. Confirm it is `(last.rate - first.rate)` regardless of n, and confirm `last` and `first` refer to `runIds[0]` and `runIds[runIds.length - 1]`. (User's prior knowledge suggests this is the case.)
2. **Confirm the UI**: read `compare/+page.svelte` to understand what is displayed. Today the per-cell text is `{counts[1]}/{n} flagged` (so users see "3/6 flagged" vs "9/17 flagged" directly in the cell). What is displayed as the "delta"? Is it a number, a percentage, an arrow? Is the n visible in the delta widget itself or only in the cell? Quote the exact rendering code.
3. **Compute the worked example**: for `3/6 vs 9/17`:
   - 3/6 = 50.0%
   - 9/17 = 52.9%
   - Rate delta = +2.9 percentage points (rounds to "+3 pp")
   - Absolute count delta = +6 flagged (3 → 9) but on different denominators
   - Confidence intervals for these two proportions (Wilson or normal approx) overlap massively; the difference is not statistically distinguishable from zero
4. **Identify the user-facing failure modes**:
   - The "+3 pp" delta looks like the policy got worse, but it could also be that the test set re-stratified and a behavior category sampled more aggressively in run B picked up more flagged cases by composition, not by policy regression.
   - If a behavior has n=6 in one run and n=17 in another (which happens when test_set is regenerated across runs, or when runs use different stratifier configurations), comparing rates without a confidence-interval treatment is misleading.
   - With small n (e.g., 6 samples), the rate is noisy: a 95% Wilson CI for 3/6 is roughly [22%, 78%]. Any rate delta within that band is statistical noise.
5. **Trace the sample provenance**: under what conditions can two runs in the same suite have different n for the same behavior? Read `assert_eval/core/artifact_cache.py` (lines 100-200 are relevant) and `assert_eval/stages/test_set.py` to understand whether the test_set fingerprint guarantees same-n across runs of the same suite. If two configs with different `target.callable` cause a test_set cache miss + regeneration, the n could differ. Today most demos cache-hit (Chang verified earlier today that PR #88's variant-a and variant-b share `v0001`), but a customer changing dimensions or sample_size between runs would split the cache and see n-disparity.
6. **Audit PR #160 specifically**: PR #160 is a 13-line patch that adds a `runLabel` helper and changes 3 render sites. Does it touch the delta logic at all? Should the delta-correctness concern hold up PR #160's merge, or is it a separate issue?
7. **Recommendation**: write a clear recommendation covering:
   - Should we hold PR #160 (likely no — it doesn't touch the delta)?
   - What is the actual bug class (math is right, UI is misleading without n and CI; or math is subtly wrong because…)?
   - Concrete viewer UX fixes to file as follow-up issues. Examples to consider:
     - Always show the per-cell n inline in compare table (already done in the cell)
     - Show n in the delta widget itself when n differs by more than X% across runs
     - Add a Wilson-CI bar to small-n rates
     - Surface a "n mismatch" warning at the top of the compare page when any behavior has > 20% variation in n across runs
     - Change the delta from "rate Δ" to "rate Δ ± CI" or to "rate Δ (n_a / n_b)"
     - Use Z-test / chi-square for "is this delta statistically significant" rather than just printing pp
   - What should the launch story say about this? If the demo we ship in PR #88 / #159 has matched n across all runs (cache-shared test_set), then the launch is fine and the issue is a post-launch viewer polish.
   - Concrete YAML / code example of when the n-disparity arises so we can spot it in real customer workflows.

## Deliverables

Write a single markdown report:

1. **TL;DR** (3-5 lines): is PR #160 affected, what is the actual issue, what to file as follow-up.
2. **The math**: walk through `(3/6) vs (9/17)` and explain why the current "rate delta" is mathematically correct but UX-incomplete.
3. **Confidence-interval context**: compute Wilson 95% CI for 3/6 and 9/17 and demonstrate that the +3pp delta is not statistically distinguishable from zero.
4. **When does n-disparity arise?** With concrete YAML / config examples from the assert_eval codebase.
5. **Today's compare-page UI**: quote what's actually shown to the user for the cell and the delta. Is n visible? Is rate visible?
6. **PR #160 verdict**: does this concern block merge? Yes/no with one-line rationale.
7. **Follow-up issue templates**: write 1-3 GitHub-issue-ready markdown templates the parent can post on `responsibleai/ASSERT` to file the actual viewer UX fixes. Include title, body, suggested labels, and acceptance criteria.

### Constraints

- Do NOT edit any file or post any GitHub comment. Audit-only.
- Do NOT modify PR #160. Just read it.
- Use Windows backslash paths when referencing local files.
- Length budget: 1500-3500 words. Math-heavy is fine.
- When done, **save the report to `C:\Users\changliu2\.copilot\session-state\7bd9ebe1-87c6-4cb9-9b92-88512f2aa196\files\pr160-delta-semantics-audit.md`**. If file writes are blocked, return the full markdown inline.
- When done, **also run this exact SQL via the sql tool to mark the todo done**: `UPDATE todos SET status = 'done' WHERE id = 'audit-160-delta'`. If you cannot reach the sql tool, mention this explicitly in your response so the parent can do it.
- When you reply to the parent, return: (1) a 4-line summary with the verdict on whether PR #160 ships as-is, (2) the saved file path (or "inline" if returned), (3) the top 2 follow-up issue titles you drafted, (4) confirmation of the SQL update.
