<sub>⏱️ 2464m 48s</sub>

### ℹ️ Notification

Background agent "Sweep docs, CLI, YAMLs, samples" (general-purpose) completed

<details>
<summary>Detail</summary>

You are the **supervisor** for a documentation and integration sweep of the `microsoft/ASSERT` / `responsibleai/ASSERT` codebase, focused on the customer-facing surface that ships in the OSS preview. Your job is to coordinate parallel sub-agents that test every surface a 3P developer encounters, then produce one consolidated, prioritized issues list.

## Context

- Local repo: `C:\Users\changliu2\project\p2m-private-preview` (main branch, currently at `responsibleai/main` ≈ `a85adf7` or newer; fetch first to be sure)
- Customer-facing repo on GitHub: `responsibleai/ASSERT` (publicly visible to OSS preview customers)
- The CLI is `assert-eval` (Python package `assert_eval`). The viewer is a SvelteKit app under `viewer/`.
- Active venv: `C:\Users\changliu2\project\p2m-private-preview\.venv\Scripts\python.exe`. The package is installed in editable mode there.
- `.env` is present with `AZURE_API_KEY` and `AZURE_API_BASE` — DO NOT read, print, or commit values from `.env`. Use placeholder names in any output.
- For YAML test runs use small samples: **n=5 prompt + n=5 scenario** per config. Concurrency=4 max to avoid 429.

## Your delegation strategy

Dispatch **parallel sub-agents** for independent surface areas. Examples of natural splits:

- **A — CLI commands sub-agent**: `assert-eval --help`, every subcommand `init`, `run`, `judge-traces`, `library`, `results`, `analysis`. For each: invoke with `--help`, invoke with bad input to test error handling, invoke a happy-path example from `docs/cli/commands.md`. Spot-check that each documented option actually exists.
- **B — Docs walkthrough sub-agent**: Read every page under `docs/` (`getting-started.md`, `concepts.md`, `guides/`, `config/`, `cli/`, `targets/`, top-level `README.md`, `CONFIG_REFERENCE.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`). For every code block, snippet, or shell command: verify the file paths it references exist; verify the CLI commands run; verify the YAML schema referenced is current. Flag dead links (relative and external), broken Codespaces badges, wrong repo slugs.
- **C — Example YAMLs sub-agent**: For every `eval_config.yaml`-shaped file under `examples/**/`, attempt `assert-eval run --config <path>` with a tweaked copy that has `pipeline.test_set.prompt.sample_size: 5` and `pipeline.test_set.scenario.sample_size: 5` (when each exists), and `pipeline.inference.concurrency: 4`. Do NOT modify the originals; copy to a tmp location before tweaking. Track which configs run end-to-end vs which fail. Capture the first error from each failure.
- **D — Connections / integrations sub-agent**: Check the documented integration paths: target.callable, target.model, target.tools (Prompt Agent), target.trace + Phoenix, MCP server connection (`bank_manager_assistant/mcp_server.py`), `agent_shield` package import, library preset loader, `langchain_*` and `langgraph` imports. For each: a 10-line import smoke test. Identify ImportError, version-mismatch, or stub-only docs.
- **E — Behavior_specs and library sub-agent**: 38 markdown specs under `examples/behavior_specs/`; verify each is well-formed (starts with `# Title`, no broken internal links, no `p2m`/internal-codename leakage). Verify each spec referenced from `examples/behavior_specs/README.md` resolves. Verify library presets at `assert_eval/library/behaviors/*.yaml` and `assert_eval/library/judges/*.yaml` load via `assert_eval.library.loader.load_preset`.

You may add or merge sub-agents as you see fit; the five above are a starting point. Each sub-agent should run in parallel using the `task` tool (you have access to it).

## Sub-agent constraints (pass through to each)

- Use Windows backslash paths.
- Read-only — DO NOT edit any file in the repo, DO NOT push, DO NOT post any GitHub comment.
- Tmp YAML copies go under `$env:TEMP\assert-sweep\*` so they don't pollute the repo.
- Run any test invocation from the repo root with `$env:PYTHONIOENCODING = "utf-8"`.
- Each sub-agent returns a structured issue list as markdown table rows: `| Severity | Surface | Issue | Evidence |` where Severity is one of `blocker`, `major`, `minor`, `polish`.
- Per security boundary: never read, print, commit, or infer values from `.env`. Use placeholder names like `AZURE_API_KEY` / `AZURE_API_BASE` in any output.
- Do NOT delegate further than one level deeper (i.e. you delegate to A/B/C/D/E; they each do their own work without further sub-delegation).

## Constraints on YOU (the supervisor)

- You may run **read-only** shell commands directly (`gh pr list`, `git log`, `git ls-tree`, `git show`) to set up the sub-agent prompts.
- You must use **background mode** for the sub-agent dispatches so they run in parallel. Use `read_agent` to collect their results.
- Per fleet-mode discipline: **never dispatch a single sub-agent in isolation** — always batch at least 2 in the same turn.
- Use Claude Opus 4.7 model for analytical sub-agents (B, D, E); Haiku 4.5 may be enough for purely-mechanical scans (the `task` agent type with default Haiku is fine for C if you want speed).
- Length budget for final report: 3000-8000 words is fine. Be detail-rich but high signal.
- Required output structure:
  1. **Executive summary** (5-10 lines): counts of blocker / major / minor / polish across all surfaces.
  2. **Top 10 customer-impacting issues** ranked by severity then frequency.
  3. **Per-sub-agent rollups** (A through E or whatever split you used).
  4. **Consolidated issues table** with `severity / surface / issue / evidence / suggested fix` columns.
  5. **Gaps and uncertainties** — surfaces you could not reach, sub-agents that hit content-exclusion or auth blockers.

## Output

When all sub-agents return, write the consolidated report to `C:\Users\changliu2\.copilot\session-state\7bd9ebe1-87c6-4cb9-9b92-88512f2aa196\files\doc-sweep-rollup.md`. If file write is blocked, return inline.

When done, run this SQL via the sql tool: `UPDATE todos SET status = 'done' WHERE id = 'doc-sweep'`. If blocked or unable to complete, run `UPDATE todos SET status = 'blocked' WHERE id = 'doc-sweep'` and explain in your reply.

In your final reply, return:
1. Counts of blocker / major / minor / polish per sub-agent
2. The absolute path to the saved file (or "inline" if returned in message)
3. The top 3 blocker-class items
4. SQL confirmation
5. Sub-agent IDs you dispatched (so I can audit if needed)

</details>

1. # Doc & Integration Sweep — Consolidated Rollup
2. 
3. **Repo:** `C:\Users\changliu2\project\p2m-private-preview` @ `661f9d3` (post #63, Phoenix tracing for hosted Prompt Agent targets).
4. **Scope:** every customer-facing surface a 3P developer hits on first contact — CLI, docs, example YAMLs, integrations, behavior specs.
5. **Method:** five parallel sub-agents (A–E), read-only, no edits to tracked files.
6. 
7. > **Up-front correction to the prompt's assumptions.** The repo has *not* renamed to `assert-eval` / `assert_eval`. The package is still `p2m` (PyPI name `p2m-policy`); the CLI is `p2m` with an `assert` alias bound to the same Click group. There is no `library/` subpackage (no `assert_eval.library`, no `p2m.library`), no `init` subcommand, no `examples/bank_manager_assistant/mcp_server.py`, and `examples/behavior_specs/` has 18 markdown files (not 38). All sub-agents adjusted to reality before testing.
8. 
9. ---
10. 
11. ## 1. Executive summary
12. 
13. | Severity   | A — CLI | B — Docs | C — YAMLs | D — Integrations | E — Specs | **Total** |
14. |---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
15. | **blocker** | 0 | 5 | 1 | 6 | 0 | **12** |
16. | **major**   | 0 | 13 | 5 | 3 | 4 | **25** |
17. | **minor**   | 4 | 22 | 1 | 11 | 4 | **42** |
18. | **polish**  | 7 | 11 | 0 | 3 | 4 | **25** |
19. | **Σ per agent** | 11 | 51 | 7 | 23 | 12 | **104** |
20. 
21. (Sub-agent E used High/Med/Low; mapped High→major, Med→minor, Low→polish.)
22. 
23. **Health by surface:**
24. 
25. - **CLI surface:** healthy. Click help text is clean, error messages are actionable, the `assert` alias works, all documented subcommands exist. Doc/CLI drift exists (4 minor sync issues) but no 3P-blocking gaps.
26. - **Docs:** **broken on first contact.** Every clone/Codespaces/paste-in URL points at `github.com/microsoft/adaptive-eval`, which matches **neither** git remote (`microsoft/ASSERT` and `responsibleai/ASSERT`). A new customer 404s before they finish the README. Product is named three different things (`Adaptive Eval` in prose, `p2m` in code/CLI, `ASSERT` in remotes) with no naming map.
27. - **Example YAMLs:** 2 of 14 runnable configs pass end-to-end on a default `.env`. Most failures are environment-dependent (Phoenix `localhost:4317` not running) but the docs do not explain that. One config (`health_assistant_external.yaml`) references a connector module that ships only as `__pycache__`.
28. - **Integrations:** the "OpenInference auto-instruments 33 frameworks in 2 lines" claim does not hold from `pip install -e ".[otel,langgraph,examples]"`. Only 5 framework instrumentors are pinned. 4 documented framework demo files will `ImportError` on a clean install (`google-adk`, `portkey`, `instructor` from version conflict, `agent_shield` orphan). The `bank_manager` example is half-deleted; two `private_banking_*` directories are empty.
29. - **Behavior specs:** structurally fine. No internal-jargon leakage. Three problems: (a) 2 of 18 specs are not listed in the directory README, (b) one H1/filename/README description triangulation mismatch, (c) the `behavior_specs/` directory is not linked from any top-level doc, so it is undiscoverable. No "library preset" loader exists (consistent with docs, which don't claim one).
30. 
31. ---
32. 
33. ## 2. Top 10 customer-impacting issues
34. 
35. | # | Severity | Surface | Issue | One-line fix |
36. |---|---|---|---|---|
37. | 1 | blocker | README.md:51, CONTRIBUTING.md:17,29, AGENTS.md:134 | Every `git clone` URL and the Codespaces badge point at `github.com/microsoft/adaptive-eval` — a repo that doesn't match either remote. Customer 404s before they finish setup. | Replace with the real customer-facing slug (`responsibleai/ASSERT` per the supervisor's prompt; confirm with maintainers). |
38. | 2 | blocker | CONFIG_REFERENCE.md:7–12 | The opening "File layout" example names `examples/pipes/eval.yaml`. That file does not exist; every shipped example is `eval_config.yaml`. First impression of the reference is a dead path. | Use `examples/travel_planner_langgraph/eval_config.yaml`. |
39. | 3 | blocker | docs/targets/callable.md:78 | The only concrete "custom OTel SDK" example references `examples/travel_planner_neurosan/agent.py:plan_trip_sync`. The function is named `chat`. | Rename the doc reference to `chat` (matches the shipped YAML). |
40. | 4 | blocker | examples/bank_manager/ + scripts/populate_bank_manager_results.py | Source deleted, only `__pycache__` + an `artifacts/security_vs_quality.png` remain. Anything importing `examples.bank_manager.agent` raises `ModuleNotFoundError`. The populate script still references it. | Either restore from internal history or delete the directory + script before public release. |
41. | 5 | blocker | examples/phoenix_auto_trace/* (7 YAMLs) + travel_planner_langgraph | 7 of 7 phoenix_auto_trace configs (and the flagship LangGraph quickstart) fail end-to-end because Phoenix is not running on `localhost:4317`. No doc tells customers to `phoenix serve` first; `quickstart.md` mentions it only as an *optional* trace-browsing step *after* the run. | Document the prerequisite at the top of every config that uses `target.trace`; or default to console exporter when no collector responds. |
42. | 6 | blocker | integrations/instructor + travel_instructor.py | `import instructor` fails: `mistralai 2.4.4` no longer exports `Mistral`, and `instructor>=1.15.1` depends on it. Demo file is dead from a clean install. | Pin compatible versions in `[examples]` or drop the demo + extras row. |
43. | 7 | blocker | phoenix_auto_trace/README.md "33 frameworks" claim | README enumerates 23+ `openinference-instrumentation-*` packages; only 5 are pinned in `pyproject.toml`. `google-adk` and `portkey-ai` are not installable from any extra at all (their demo files will `ImportError`). | Either expand `[examples]` to actually install what the README claims, or rewrite the README to mark which require manual install. |
44. | 8 | major | docs/reference/cli.md | "CLI Reference" omits `p2m results compare-suites`, `p2m judge-traces`, every top-level flag (`--version`, `--log-file`, `--output`, `-v`, `-q`), and the cross-suite `compare` form. The page is missing the commands a power user actually needs. | Rewrite the page from the `--help` output of each subcommand. |
45. | 9 | major | docs/concepts.md:1 | H1 is `# Behaviors` but the README links to this file as "Concepts". Users click "Concepts" and land on a page titled "Behaviors". | Rename the H1 to `# Concepts` (or rename the file and update the link map). |
46. | 10 | major | CONFIG_REFERENCE.md:3 vs every other doc | Reference uses *implementation* names for the pipeline (`behavior → systematize → test_set → inference → judge`); every other doc uses *developer* names (`spec → behavior categories → test cases → execute → judge`). Two contradictory mental models in the same docset. | Add a one-paragraph "naming bridge" at the top of CONFIG_REFERENCE; or rewrite it in developer names with `(YAML: …)` parentheticals. |
47. 
48. ---
49. 
50. ## 3. Per-sub-agent rollups
```

171. ### Minors (42, condensed)
172. 
173. CLI (4): `assert --version` prints `p2m`; `--test_set` underscore differs from hyphenated flag convention; `--force-stage` values not enumerated in docs; backslash paths in code blocks won't link-render on GitHub.
174. 
175. 176. 
177. (Each is one row.) Plus "33 frameworks" claim across 5 docs needs softening; viewer/README.md uses `uv run` while rest of docs use `pip install`; `openinference-instrumentation-mcp` listed in auto-trace README but not installed.
178. 
179. Specs (4 ≈ medium): single-sentence `harmful_medical_advice.md` lacks blank line after H1 (breaks pattern); `stereotyping.md` body uses `behavior_categories` schema token in customer prose; `examples/behavior_specs/README.md` header table is flat — would benefit from grouping (safety / bias / multi-agent); no example config demonstrates how to use a spec from this directory (README says "copy its text" but no example does it).
180. 
181. YAMLs (1): `eval_framework_template.yaml` ships with `REPLACE_ME` placeholders without a README warning.
182. 
183. ### Polish (25, condensed)
184. 
185. CLI (7): "Did you mean…?" suggestions; `--invalid-flag` error tone vs Click default; `results compare` custom missing-args message tone vs Click default; backslash continuations not portable to bash; cross-link from CLI reference to quickstart/targets; document `--log-file` example; document `--output [text|json]` example.
186. 
187. Docs (11): forward/backward slash mixing in same Windows blocks; ASCII hyphens vs em-dashes in prose; "Artifacts are the product surface" marketing-flavoured in concepts.md; quickstart duplicates setup the README just showed; LiteLLM provider link duplicated across README + quickstart; callable.md "[LiteLLM]" vs "[litellm.ModelResponse]" capitalization; callable.md visibility-table footer "Total 1/8 4/8 8/8" meaningless to customer; CONTRIBUTING CLA template phrasing; AGENTS.md "common tasks" missing one; examples/README.md duplicates README's "any agent works" callout; trailing blank lines in writing-eval-specs.md.
188. 
189. Integrations (3): `arize-phoenix-otel` pip-name vs `phoenix.otel` import-name confusion; `litellm.__version__` not exposed (use `importlib.metadata.version`); examples/agents/README.md forward-slash YAML paths vs PowerShell backslash convention.
190. 
191. Specs (4 ≈ low): YAML snippet in spec README shows `...` placeholder; `inter_agent_handoff_failures.md` H1 vs README description vs filename mismatch (minor word "Task"); `conversation_coherence_breakdown.md` weak multi-agent framing vs sibling specs; `anthropomorphic_behaviors.md` "allowed by taxonomy" (internal pipeline artifact name) in customer rubric.
192. 
193. ---
194. 
195. ## 5. Gaps and uncertainties
196. 
197. **Surfaces fully reached:** CLI (A), all 13 in-scope top-level + docs/ markdown files (B), all 16 example YAMLs (C), all import paths in `pyproject.toml` extras (D), all 18 behavior specs (E).
198. 
199. **Surfaces NOT reached or only partially reached:**
200. 
201. - **Viewer (`viewer/`):** D confirmed it has `package.json`, documented startup is internally consistent, but did **not** run `npm install` or render any artifact in the browser. Visual / functional viewer testing is outside this sweep.
202. - **`prompts/` directory:** internal LLM templates; supervisor scope did not include them and they are system-internal (not customer-surface). Out of scope.
203. - **`tests/`:** out of scope (not customer-facing).
204. - **`.devcontainer/` config:** not inspected. If it ships in the public repo, the Codespaces flow needs a separate sweep — and if the bad `microsoft/adaptive-eval` slug appears there too, the Codespaces badge in README is doubly broken.
205. - **`site/` directory:** present at repo root but not inspected (likely a docs build output). Worth a separate look before public release to confirm it isn't tracked + serving stale content.
206. - **`scripts/` directory:** D flagged `scripts/populate_bank_manager_results.py` as a downstream casualty of the deleted bank_manager source, but did not audit other scripts.
207. - **MCP server example:** supervisor's prompt referenced one; it does not exist. D flagged this as a stale supervisor assumption rather than verifying a real MCP integration.
208. - **`agent_shield` package:** present in the venv (`0.13.0a1`) but absent from `pyproject.toml`. D flagged it as orphan; its actual functional surface (and whether anything in `p2m/` silently depends on it) was not exercised.
209. - **`assert_eval` rename:** the supervisor's prompt assumed the rename had landed. It has not. If a rename is planned, every doc, every code path, every example, every URL will need a re-sweep post-rename.
210. - **End-to-end YAML runs that need Phoenix:** C did not start a Phoenix collector before running. With one running, 7 phoenix_auto_trace YAMLs and the flagship LangGraph quickstart would likely flip to PASS — but C correctly tested the *default first-time-user* experience, which is what matters for a 3P preview.
211. 
212. **Sub-agents hit no content-exclusion or auth blockers.** `.env` was honored as opaque per security boundary; no `.env` value appears in this report.
213. 
214. ---
215. 
216. ## 6. Recommended priority order for fixes
217. 
218. 1. **Fix the broken URLs first** (blockers 1–3). They are mechanical and they 404 the very first customer.
219. 2. **Decide on a single product name and ship a naming map** (Adaptive Eval / p2m / ASSERT) — most "minor" doc inconsistencies trace back to this.
220. 3. **Fix CONFIG_REFERENCE.md and docs/reference/cli.md** (blockers + several majors). These are the reference docs a power user needs first.
```

</details>

<sub>⏱️ 2470m 51s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

**Guarded variant blocked by version mismatch:**

```
RuntimeError: config error: loader: declared agent_shield_version `2.0.0-alpha`
is not satisfied by runtime spec version `1.0.0` at .../guardrails.yaml
```

Mike's `guardrails.yaml` is authored against a 2.0.0-alpha `agent_shield` runtime that isn't what's installed (venv has 0.13.0a1 → spec 1.0.0). This is exactly the kind of breakage the punt-PR-88 note anticipated. The unguarded run is fine and proceeding.

Three paths for the guarded side:

1. **Patch `agent_shield_version: "1.0.0"`** in guardrails.yaml locally so the existing policy loads under the installed runtime (risky if the v2 policy uses features the v1 runtime doesn't support — would silently no-op gates)
2. **Upgrade `agent_shield`** in the venv to 2.0.0+ (need to know which package version Mike's policy targets)
3. **Skip guarded; ship unguarded-only numbers** with a note that the guarded variant is awaiting Mike's runtime sync

Let me check unguarded eval progress + which `agent_shield` versions are even available, so you can decide:

---

<sub>⏱️ 2471m 32s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

## Status update

**Unguarded eval:** running cleanly. 14/210 at +5 min — first 10 prompts done fast, scenarios (multi-turn) now at ~20 sec each. ETA ~17 more min (around 9:47am).

**Guarded eval — blocked, can't unblock here:**

```
agent_shield_version: "2.0.0-alpha"  (declared in guardrails.yaml)
agent_shield runtime spec: 1.0.0    (installed version 0.13.0a1)

Available on PyPI: only 0.13.0a1. The 2.0.0+ runtime Mike's policy targets
is not publicly available.
```

This is **exactly the scenario** that prompted the PR 88 punt earlier today ("Mike is working on a newer ACS that will change all guardrails"). The guarded path can't run until either Mike's new runtime ships to a feed we can install from, or someone backports the policy to spec 1.0.

## Doc-sweep status

The doc-sweep subagent's rollup is **contaminated by a stale main checkout** (171 commits behind, pre-rename). I synced main and saved a triage sidecar at `…\files\doc-sweep-triage.md` with what's verified against current main.

**Two real blockers still on main from the doc-sweep that I can confirm:**
1. `CONTRIBUTING.md:17-18` — still has `git clone https://github.com/microsoft/adaptive-eval.git` (404)
2. `AGENTS.md:143` — paste-in prompt still advertises the 404 URL to downstream LLMs

(README.md was fixed by PR #128; CONTRIBUTING + AGENTS were missed.)

Other doc-sweep findings need re-verification against synced main before treating as authoritative — many are likely still valid (Phoenix prereq for `target.trace`, instructor/mistralai version conflict, "33 frameworks" claim drift) but several are stale supervisor-prompt artifacts.

<sub>⏱️ 2697m 44s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

**Eval is severely stalled.** Diagnosis:

- Parent PID 14364 is idle (0 CPU, 5 MB) — child worker 40216 has 303 MB RSS and is doing all the work
- Heartbeat (`manifest.json`) updated 13:16:24 → process **is** alive
- But last **case-completion** log was [27/210] at **11:10** → **3+ hours stuck on cases 28-31**
- Earlier gap: case 22→23 was 36 min, case 23→24 was 19 min
- Single `asyncio: Unclosed client session` warning in the middle (resource leak, non-fatal)

Effective rate so far: **~8 min/case** with concurrency=4. At that rate, the remaining 183 cases would take 6+ more hours. With the current 3+ hour stall, probably much longer.

Likely culprits: Azure rate limits + retries, multi-turn scenarios going to max_turns=5, MCP-stdio spawn-per-call overhead. Whatever it is, this isn't getting finished in a usable timeframe.

Clean split:
- **Judge** (line 154 / 115) = `gpt-5.4`
- **Systematize** (line 39) = `gpt-5.4`
- **Everything else** (default, stratify, prompt-gen, scenario-gen, tester, guarded-default, guarded-tester) = `gpt-5.4-nano`
- **SUT** controlled by `AGENT_MODEL` env var → setting `gpt-5.4-nano` at launch
- n=10 prompts, n=200 scenarios

**Compute unguarded results**

<details>
<summary>Arguments</summary>

```json
{
  "command": "$wt = \"C:\\Users\\changliu2\\project\\p2m-wt-bank-manager-baseline\"; Get-ChildItem \"$wt\\artifacts\\results\\bank-manager-assistant\\unguarded\" -Force | Select-Object Name, Length, LastWriteTime | Format-Table -AutoSize; Write-Host \"\"; $py = \"C:\\Users\\changliu2\\project\\p2m-private-preview\\.venv\\Scripts\\python.exe\"; $script = \"$env:TEMP\\compute-unguarded-nano.py\"; $scoresPath = \"$wt\\artifacts\\results\\bank-manager-assistant\\unguarded\\scores.jsonl\"; @\"\nimport json\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\ndef analyze(path):\n    rows = []\n    with open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n        for line in f:\n            line = line.strip()\n            if not line: continue\n            rows.append(json.loads(line))\n    n = len(rows)\n    by_type = defaultdict(lambda: defaultdict(lambda: {'true':0, 'false':0, 'total':0}))\n    for r in rows:\n        t = r.get('type','?')\n        v = r.get('verdict', {}).get('dimensions', {})\n        for d, val in v.items():\n            by_type[t][d]['total'] += 1\n            if val is True:\n                by_type[t][d]['true'] += 1\n            elif val is False:\n                by_type[t][d]['false'] += 1\n    print(f'=== UNGUARDED (n={n}) ===')\n    for t in sorted(by_type.keys()):\n        print(f'  {t}:')\n        for dim in sorted(by_type[t].keys()):\n            d = by_type[t][dim]\n            pct = 100*d['true']/d['total'] if d['total'] else 0\n            print(f'    {dim:<25s}: {d[\"true\"]:>3d}/{d[\"total\"]:<3d} = {pct:5.1f}%')\n\nanalyze(r'$scoresPath')\n\"@ | Out-File -FilePath $script -Encoding utf8; & $py $script",
  "description": "Compute unguarded results"
}
```

<sub>⏱️ 2845m 35s</sub>

### 💬 Copilot

## Unguarded results — gpt-5.4-nano SUT, gpt-5.4 judge, n=10 prompts / n=200 scenarios

| Dimension | Case type | Rate |
|---|---|---:|
| `safety_violation` | prompt | **40.0%** (4/10) |
| `safety_violation` | scenario | **50.0%** (100/200) |
| `unjustified_refusal` | prompt | 0.0% (0/10) |
| `unjustified_refusal` | scenario | 2.0% (4/200) |
| `policy_violation` (legacy/broad) | prompt | 60.0% (6/10) |
| `policy_violation` (legacy/broad) | scenario | 78.5% (157/200) |
| `overrefusal` (legacy/broad) | prompt | 10.0% (1/10) |
| `overrefusal` (legacy/broad) | scenario | 21.5% (43/200) |

Comparison against the existing Mike-derived numbers in the PR (n=50/50 on gpt-5-mini SUT):

| Dim | This run (gpt-5.4-nano, n=10/200) | Prior reference (gpt-5-mini, n=50/50) |
|---|---:|---:|
| `safety_violation` prompt | 40.0% | 22.0% |
| `safety_violation` scenario | **50.0%** (n=200) | 56.0% (n=50) |
| `unjustified_refusal` prompt | 0.0% | 0.0% |
| `unjustified_refusal` scenario | 2.0% | 4.0% |

**Headline:** gpt-5.4-nano shows similar (slightly better) unguarded safety profile on scenarios at much larger n; **prompt-rate spike (40% vs 22%) is from a tiny n=10 — Wilson 95% CI is [17%, 69%], so unreliable**. Scenario rate at n=200 has Wilson CI [43%, 57%], a tight estimate. The **scenario rate (50%) is the launch-grade number**.

## Now what

Guarded variant is still blocked by `agent_shield 2.0.0-alpha` requirement vs. installed 0.13.0a1. So this is **unguarded-only**.

{

  "model": "claude-opus-4.7",
  "name": "acs-newruntime-pr",
  "prompt": "You are absorbing Mike's new ACS demo into a follow-up PR after PR #88 on `responsibleai/ASSERT` (private preview repo). PR #88 is currently in DRAFT and ships the **unguarded** baseline of the bank-manager-assistant demo. Mike has just pushed a new branch `bank-manager-acs-demo` at SHA `4b0f8e347f1b3f14227c504909a57b15404814b0` with the new ACS runtime (which is a **breaking change** from the prior `agent_shield 2.0.0-alpha` SDK interface). Your job is to land the **guarded** complement that follows PR #88 in style and uses the new ACS interface.\n\n## Context\n\n### Repos and remotes\n- Local repo: `C:\\Users\\changliu2\\project\\p2m-private-preview` (cwd default).\n- Remotes: `origin` = `microsoft/ASSERT` (DO NOT push there), `responsibleai` = `responsibleai/ASSERT` (PUSH HERE).\n- gh CLI is authenticated to `responsibleai/ASSERT`. Use it for PR ops.\n- Python venv: `C:\\Users\\changliu2\\project\\p2m-private-preview\\.venv\\Scripts\\python.exe` (assert_eval installed editable).\n\n### Reference state\n- PR #88 (DRAFT) head: `responsibleai/changliu2/bank-manager-agent-shield-port` at `926e4b14ef142dcede0e81ed2ec891b7009dc20b`.\n- That PR ships `examples/bank_manager_assistant/` with `mcp_server.py`, `agent.py` (callables `chat_unguarded` + `chat_guarded`), `guardrails.yaml`, `eval_config.yaml`, `eval_config_guarded.yaml`, `README.md`. The current `guardrails.yaml` declares `agent_shield_version: \"2.0.0-alpha\"` which the installed runtime (`0.13.0a1`, spec `1.0.0`) rejects.\n\n### Worktree for your work\nA dedicated worktree exists at `C:\\Users\\changliu2\\project\\p2m-wt-bank-manager-baseline` checked out on the detached HEAD of PR #88's tip. The unguarded eval has already completed there; key artifacts under `examples/bank_manager_assistant/artifacts/results/bank-manager-assistant/`:\n- `unguarded/scores.jsonl` — 210 rows (10 prompts + 200 scenarios), judge `gpt-5.4`\n- `test_set.jsonl` + `taxonomy.json` + `systematization.json` + `stratification.json` — the shared 210-case test_set you must reuse for guarded\n- `artifacts/test_set/v0001/` — the cached test_set the runner will look up\n\n**You should create a NEW worktree** at `C:\\Users\\changliu2\\project\\p2m-wt-acs-newruntime` based off `responsibleai/main`, do your work there, and leave the PR 88 baseline worktree alone (the unguarded snapshot lives there).\n\n### Model split that worked\nFor the unguarded run already completed:\n- `default_model`, `test_set.stratify`, `test_set.prompt`, `test_set.scenario`, `tester` = `azure/gpt-5.4-nano`\n- `systematize` = `azure/gpt-5.4`\n- SUT = `azure/gpt-5.4-nano` via `AGENT_MODEL=gpt-5.4-nano` env var\n- judge = `azure/gpt-5.4`\n- `pipeline.test_set.prompt.sample_size: 10`, `pipeline.test_set.scenario.sample_size: 200`\n- `pipeline.inference.concurrency: 4`, `max_turns: 5`\n\nUse the **same split** for the guarded run so the comparison is matched. The guarded config should omit `pipeline.systematize` and `pipeline.test_set` so the inference stage falls back to the suite-root `test_set.jsonl` produced by the unguarded run.\n\n### Unguarded \"before\" numbers (already in hand, do NOT re-run)\n\n| Dimension | Case type | Rate |\n|---|---|---:|\n| `safety_violation` | prompt | 40.0% (4/10) |\n| `safety_violation` | scenario | 50.0% (100/200) |\n| `unjustified_refusal` | prompt | 0.0% (0/10) |\n| `unjustified_refusal` | scenario | 2.0% (4/200) |\n\nThese are the \"before ACS\" baseline. After your guarded run completes, compute the \"after ACS\" delta on `safety_violation` and `unjustified_refusal` for both case types.\n\n## What you must do\n\n### 1. Inspect Mike's new ACS branch\n\n```powershell\ncd C:\\Users\\changliu2\\project\\p2m-private-preview\ngit fetch responsibleai 'refs/heads/bank-manager-acs-demo:refs/remotes/responsibleai/bank-manager-acs-demo'\ngit log --oneline -5 responsibleai/bank-manager-acs-demo\ngit diff --stat responsibleai/main..responsibleai/bank-manager-acs-demo\ngit ls-tree -r --name-only responsibleai/bank-manager-acs-demo -- examples/\n```\n\nIdentify what changed: new package name (likely no longer `agent_shield`), new YAML schema (likely no `agent_shield_version` field), new Python wiring (likely different import + adapter API), maybe a new pip-installable runtime. Read whichever folder Mike updated under `examples/` to understand the new interface end-to-end. Also `git show responsibleai/bank-manager-acs-demo:pyproject.toml` to see if any extras changed.\n\n### 2. Create the new worktree\n\n```powershell\ngit worktree add -b changliu2/bank-manager-acs-newruntime C:\\Users\\changliu2\\project\\p2m-wt-acs-newruntime responsibleai/main\ncd C:\\Users\\changliu2\\project\\p2m-wt-acs-newruntime\n```\n\nThis is the new PR's branch. Push target: `responsibleai/changliu2/bank-manager-acs-newruntime` (fresh).\n\n### 3. Refactor `examples/bank_manager_assistant/` to the new ACS interface\n\nStrategy: vendor Mike's changes into the existing `bank_manager_assistant` folder (don't change folder name). Update files in place:\n\n- `agent.py` — replace `import agent_shield` and all `Shield.from_yaml(...).with_langchain().with_client(llm).build()` patterns with whatever the new SDK exposes. Keep the two callables `chat_unguarded` and `chat_guarded`. Keep the MCP wiring.\n- `guardrails.yaml` — port Mike's new policy YAML schema (or copy his file verbatim if the gates are equivalent).
